title: sales development intern
description:
we are hiring! the aro business unit at ingersoll rand seeks a sales development intern to support our sales enablement team in bryan ohio. this hands-on summer internship invites you to dive into the heart of our sales enablement team and immerse yourself in the dynamic world of sales.

job summary

the sales development intern will play a pivotal role in lead qualification, ensuring our sales team is equipped with a robust pipeline of opportunities to grow the business. this isn't just about making calls; it's about shaping strategies, refining tools, and deploying resources to maximize sales productivity and effectiveness.

throughout this internship, you'll gain invaluable skills in sales excellence; from lead qualification techniques to process optimization and collaborative stakeholder engagement. this experience will provide developing and implementing strategies that drive impactful business outcomes for aro.

join us and empower our sales force for success while gaining hands-on experience and shaping the future of sales enablement at aro!

key position responsibilities

lead management: the process of categorizing and engaging with inbound leads promptly and efficiently based on predefined criteria.

classify inbound leads based on needs, budget, and decision-making capabilities.ensure prompt engagement by working through the inbound lead queue via phone calls and emails within a 4-hour timeframe.efficiently route leads to sales partners based on region, product, and other qualifying attributes.

sales process optimization: the analysis and enhancement of sales procedures to improve efficiency and effectiveness.

analyze sales processes to identify areas for improvement.collaborate with team to implement enhancements and streamline workflows, ultimately boosting operational efficiency.

content development: creation of engaging resources aligned with brand messaging and sales objectives.

partner with the team to create compelling sales collateral, presentations, and training materials.ensure all content aligns with brand messaging and sales objectives.

standard work: establishment and documentation of consistent procedures to ensure efficiency and effectiveness in lead management and other tasks.

establish, update and document lead management standard work to ensure consistency and efficiency.assist in training on processes, tools, and templates.develop scripting for frequently asked questions.

documentation: organizing and maintaining accurate records of lead status and actions taken for reference and follow-up.

organize, maintain, and update lead status in the lead generation system.review lead status and follow up with sales on leads not acted on according to process metrics and detractor ratings.

reporting and analytics: tracking, analyzing, and reporting on key performance indicators to identify trends and inform strategic decision-making.

assist in tracking and analyzing key performance indicators/metrics (kpi’s).generate reports/dashboards to provide insights into sales effectiveness, identifying areas for growth.collaborate with stakeholders to determine countermeasures for unfavorable metrics.

work arrangement and travel requirements

this is an onsite internship located at the aro office in bryan oh. 

key qualifications

undergraduate student, at least sophomore level, currently pursuing a bachelor’s degree in business, communications, marketing, or a related field at ability to work at least 20 hours per week, up to 40 hours per week. strong communication skills both written and on phone.high level of organization and time management skillslistens and responds in professional manner.3.0 cumulative gpa

title: intern, legends college
description:
legends

founded in 2008, legends is a premium experiences company with more than 1,500 full-time and 30,000 seasonal team members globally. legends has six divisions operating worldwide - global planning, global sales, hospitality, global partnerships, global merchandise, and global technology solutions - offering clients and partners a 360-degree service solution platform to elevate their brand and execute their vision. currently, legends works with marquee clients across business verticals including professional sports; collegiate; attractions; entertainment; and conventions and leisure. we are the industry leaders in designing, planning, and realizing exceptional experiences in sports and entertainment.

venue/project description

syracuse athletics and legends announced a 10-year partnership that will integrate data and analytics, marketing, ‘cuse athletics fund annual giving, premium seating, and general ticket sales initiatives into a single revenue-driven approach. legends will support the ‘cuse athletics fund annual fundraising program. legends’ unique fundraising platform, an essential service embraced by higher education, identifies effective fan and donor engagement strategies to amplify philanthropic giving and other opportunities. the partnership will also ensure that the orange build upon their legacy of supporting student-athletes in the classroom and on the playing fields. legends will also team with syracuse university’s falk college of sport & human dynamics to provide education, mentorship, and career opportunities to students.

the role 

this opportunity is open for candidates who can work on-site at the syracuse athletics offices from may 2024 – july 2024, with potential extension into the fall 2024 semester. hours range from 15-25 hours per week.  the intern will be focused on marketing graphics and is principally responsible for assisting our graphic designer and manger of content and strategy with conceptualizing and designing creative assets to support syracuse athletics’ sales, marketing, and philanthropic efforts. examples include: sales collateral, email graphics, social media graphics, digital ads, social ads, billboard ads, print ads and more. experience with video editing and motion graphics is a plus.

essential functions

assist with the creation and editing of graphics, videos, and other media assets for assigned marketing projects.manage content calendar and digital marketing traffic sheets.assist with managing weekly tasks within the team.organize and log content on the server as needed.able to create content that follows syracuse athletics brand guidelines.develop specific marketing copy for email, social, radio, and tv campaigns.identify and aggregate existing syracuse athletics content to leverage in marketing campaigns.available to work nights and weekends as needed to gather photos and videos at syracuse athletic events, including gamedays and special events as credentialed personnel.

qualifications

to perform this job successfully, the candidate must be able to perform each essential duty below at the highest levels. the requirements listed below are representative of the knowledge, skill, and/or ability required.

has a degree or is pursuing a degree in a major/program similar to the following: multimedia, digital media, sports marketing, broadcast journalism, videography/photography, etc.experience with adobe photoshop is required. adobe premiere, after effects, and acrobat is a plus. experience with photography and videography is a big plus.experience handling creative projects from concept to completion.an ability to deliver exceptional work by set weekly deadlines.an interest in marketing for collegiate sports.excellent interpersonal and collaborative skills.an ability to receive and implement feedback from several stakeholders.thrives in a fast-paced environment.able to work on-site at the syracuse athletics offices.

benefits

$15.00 per hourwork in a fun, exciting environment.gain hands-on experience and grow knowledge base.collaborate with experienced industry professionals.

description:
xometry (nasdaq: xmtr) powers the industries of today and tomorrow by connecting the people with big ideas to the manufacturers who can bring them to life. xometry’s digital marketplace gives manufacturers the critical resources they need to grow their business while also making it easy for buyers at fortune 1000 companies to tap into global manufacturing capacity.

2024 summer internship - accounting intern

xometry (nasdaq: xmtr) powers the industries of today and tomorrow by connecting the people with big ideas to the manufacturers who can bring them to life. xometry's digital marketplace gives manufacturers the critical resources they need to grow their business while also making it easy for buyers at fortune 1000 companies to tap into global manufacturing capacity.

xometry inc. 2024 summer internship cohort

if you are a college student who is ready to gain real world experience at one of the fastest-growing technology companies in the manufacturing space, the xometry summer internship program is for you! the internship program is designed to teach the future leaders of our industry the technical, business and communication skills necessary for success.

at xometry, our greatest assets are the world-class employees who work hard every day to uphold our reputation for innovation, reliability and relentless devotion to helping customers. for eleven weeks, you will work alongside those employees to learn the business and help solve the problems they currently experience. in addition to the daily, hands-on experience within your team, you will collaborate with your fellow interns on a case study centered around one of xometry' s business needs or challenges. at the end of the eleven-week period, you will present your findings to senior leadership as a team.

as a member of the xometry internship program within the accounting team, you can expect:

an environment that encourages entrepreneurial spirit, hands-on experience across functional areas. you will:

contribute to process improvement initiatives to enhance efficiency and effectiveness within the order to cash cycle. support the analysis of customer aging reports, dso (days sales outstanding), and other key metrics related to accounts receivable. review process of reconciling customer accounts receivable balances and resolving any outstanding issues or discrepancies. review and recommend enhancements in cash application processes, including posting customer payments and reconciling bank deposits. 

the ideal candidate will be pursuing degrees in: accounting, business, or related field

the program also includes:

mentorship from xometry managers, as well as a dedicated intern captaina culture that embraces change, speed, and flexibility in how your work is accomplishedan environment that requires adaptability to changes in the industry and advances in technologya company who values diversity, inclusion and teamwork

eligible candidates for the internship program will meet these requirements:

currently enrolled full time at an accredited college or university with plans to graduate in may 2025 (summer between junior and senior year) have a minimum of a 3.5 grade point averageability to work approximately 40 hours per week must be available to work for the entire duration of the internship program (june 10 - august 16, 2024)able to provide proof of legal right to work in the united statesability to commute to our north bethesda, md officeprovide unofficial transcript

description:
job description

the program: 

our future success depends on hiring extraordinary, early in career talent who are looking to power next-generation services and solutions. we are hiring bachelor’s and master’s degree students with a diverse set of skills and experiences to work in and across multiple teams within oracle. our program is intended to improve your overall learning experience, help build your network, and accelerate your chance to create an impact. create the future with us!

the company:

with a presence in over 175 countries, we are one of the biggest technology companies in the world. we're using innovative new technologies to tackle real-world problems today. from advancing energy efficiency to reimagining online commerce, the work we do is not only redefining the world of business—it's helping advance governments, power nonprofits, and giving billions of people the tools they need to outpace change. for more information about oracle (nyse:orcl), visit us at oracle.com.

we are seeking candidates with the expertise and passion for solving challenging unique problems. product management interns will use knowledge and creativity to guide the development of innovative new systems from the ground up.

engage with internal and external collaborators to identify problems and opportunities.passionately represent the voice of our customers to internal teams.communicate with customers about new feature rollouts, gather feedback, iterate.design solutions, write use cases, business requirements, and documentation to address problems and opportunitiesevangelize ideas from inception to launch, persuading collaborators. influence the roadmaps and backlogs of core engineering teams.present to and align with leadership collaborators.track dependencies on internal and external collaborators to ensure successful execution.partner with project and program management to align on key timelines and commitments.evaluate market trends and know the competitive landscape.

to be considered for a product manager intern position, the objective minimum qualifications (omqs) below must be met. please ensure the application clearly indicates that you meet these omqs.

will be enrolled in a bachelor’s or master’s degree program in computer science, engineering management, information systems management, business analytics, or an equivalent field during the 2024-2025 school year.have completed at least your junior year toward your undergraduate degree, or higher, by summer 2024.are proficient (e.g., can complete coding projects without any assistance) in at least one of the following programming languages & technologies:java, c, c++, python, sql, linux, git, githubhave academic course work, projects, internships, and/or research experience in one or more of the following computer science areas:artificial intelligence / machine learning / natural language processingbig data / data structures / algorithmscloud computingcomputer systems / distributed systems /embedded systems / operating systemsdatabase systems/designobject oriented designuser interface design have academic course work, work experience, or completed project research in one or more of the following business management areas (or equivalents):accountingbusiness analyticsbusiness strategyeconomicsfinancemarketingoperations researchoptimizationstatisticsattend a university in the usauthorized to work in the us in 2024available to intern june-september 2024

high-impact projects: you’ll get the opportunity to directly influence our groundbreaking products. got a question or idea? voice it. your curiosity could help advance our next-generation cloud applications and help us find new ways to apply emerging technologies like ai, machine learning, and blockchain.

mentors that care: work with industry-leading professionals and award-winning engineers. on top of your project supervisor, you’ll be assigned a peer mentor—a recent hire who can help you navigate the internship experience. you’ll also be paired with intern buddies to ensure 360 degree support and engagement.

world-class training: grow from once-in-a-lifetime learning opportunities and exposure to the latest technologies. you’ll benefit from comprehensive on-boarding and job-specific training that will help you get the most out of your time with us. leadership training will help you build your eye for business.

a fun-filled summer: enjoy a close-knit, community experience alongside your class of fellow summer interns. our interns work across the length and breadth of our business but come together for fun weekly events like tech talks, networking, volunteering, and fun social happenings.

key responsibilities • develops strong communication and teamwork skills through collaboration with youth program staff to smoothly implement daily activities and utilize provided materials effectively.• enhances problem-solving abilities by providing academic support through tutoring youth program participants in subjects such as math, english/reading, science, and social studies.• gains experience in content creation and digital outreach by assisting outreach staff in capturing engaging social media content, including photos and videos, to promote the program's dynamic nature.• refines organizational skills by supporting youth development programs (ydp) staff in preparing physical materials crucial for programming, contributing to a well-structured and stimulating environment.• engages in personal development through regular check-ins with ydp staff to discuss progress and establish goals, fostering continuous improvement and growth.• cultivates networking skills by establishing and maintaining relationships with collaborative partners, actively seeking opportunities for new partnerships to enrich the youth program.• demonstrates initiative and responsibility in maintaining a clean and organized workspace by taking the lead in cleaning up and organizing the youth program office, ensuring efficiency both during and after program hours.• exhibits a welcoming and organized approach as the designated intake person for youth program participants upon their arrival, ensuring a positive and structured start to their program experience.•abide by the conflict of interest policy as well as the code of conduct.
qualifications • demonstrates the ability to cultivate strong professional relationships within the organization and build trust with students, their families, and staff.• communicates clearly and effectively, both verbally and in writing.• exhibits strong time management and organizational skills.• capable of effective class management and conflict resolution when working with youth.• displays flexibility and dedication to meeting varying program needs.• proficient in using microsoft office suite, google docs, and canva.• shows a commitment to diversity, inclusion, and aligns with the mission of the filipino community of seattle (fcs).• possesses cross-cultural experience and sensitivity.• works collaboratively as a team member.• must be a u.s. citizen or permanent resident.• successfully completed criminal record check and child abuse registry.• strong interpersonal skills and the ability to connect with individuals of various ages and backgrounds.• passion for community engagement and a genuine interest in the well-being and development of youth.• proven ability to multitask and handle various responsibilities simultaneously.
physical demands: the physical demands described here are representative of those that must be met by an employee to successfully perform the essential functions of this job:  • occasionally involves light work: exerting up to 20 pounds of force and/or up to 10 pounds of force and/or negligible amounts of force to move objects. • occasionally requires walking for extended periods, particularly during field trips, which may include various terrains. • constantly requires the ability to give and receive detailed information through verbal communication. • constantly requires precision in working with fingers rather than the whole hand or arm. • constantly involves repetitive movement of the wrists, hands, and/or fingers, such as when using a computer or handling materials.• often requires walking or moving about to accomplish tasks within the program environment. • occasionally requires standing and/or sitting for sustained periods of time during program activities and meetings. • occasionally requires ascending or descending stairs or ramps using feet and legs and/or hands and arms, especially during field trips or within the program facility. • occasionally involves raising objects from a lower to a higher position or moving objects horizontally, requiring both upper and lower body strength.

title: strategy & marketplace intern
description:
self financial is a venture-backed, high-growth fintech company with a mission to increase economic inclusion and financial resilience by empowering people to build credit and build savings.

we're looking for people who share our passion and are driven to tackle challenges, find solutions and make the financial space better for the communities we serve.

our team is passionate about challenging the status quo of the credit industry by providing people accessible tools to take control of their credit. executing on our mission requires deep collaboration across our teams to ensure our products reach the people who can benefit from them the most, particularly the 100 million+ americans who have no or low credit.

we celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees. to that end, we seek to recruit, develop and retain the most talented people from a diverse candidate pool.

as an intern on the strategy and marketplace team, you'll support senior leaders tackling problems core to self's long-term business. you will partner across the team to support initiatives ranging from partnership identification and development to market analysis and competitive positioning

what you will do

customer discovery and analysis: conducting customer interviews or surveys to identify key pain points and trends within our existing customer baseexternal competitive analysis: putting together market maps of various niches within the fintech space. would help us understand where self fits in and what products might be a good fitproduct positioning work: given the products we have and some we are considering, we'd like to understand how to best position/frame our offerings

what will you need

prior experience working with cross-functional teams on business development and/or marketing initiativesprior experience with partnership generation (ideation to sourcing)data analysis using excel or r

benefits and perks

we have the compensation and benefits you expect. but there's one thing that self financial can offer that many companies cannot: we can positively change the world, while making a profit. we are a team of builders, empowering our customers to build their dreams. we have a do the right thing ethos in all that we do, and we hope you value that approach, too.

our perks include

 company equity in the form of rsus  quarterly performance-based bonuses  generous employer-paid health, vision and dental insurance coverage  flexible vacation policy  educational assistance  free gym membership  casual dress code  team building events and activities  remote work arrangements/ flexible work schedule  paid parental leave 

self financial requires all employees hired to successfully pass a background check.

we are an equal opportunity employer.

title: grant writing internship
description:

position type: part time, remote (must be in the state of washington)position date: 6 months, starting may going until november with possibility for extensionlocation: seattle, washington duration: part time (6-10 hours a week) 
about us: by empowering youth, the seattle student union united middle and high school students across the city, fostering collective action for tangible change. since our founding in january 2022, we have championed causes like abortion rights, climate justice, mental health, black lives matter, and gun violence prevention. through numerous walkouts and rallies, we’ve won $24 million for school mental health counselors, and a statewide assault weapons ban and we’re just getting started.. 
as we expand statewide and become the washington youth alliance, we will forge a better future - one that is more just and compassionate, where no young person is refused access to mental health services, where we address the climate crisis as a priority, and gun violence is an epidemic of the past.
internship opportunity: we are currently seeking one (1) passionate and motivated individual to join our team as a grant writing intern. this is an exciting opportunity for individuals who have a keen interest in youth activism, writing and grant writing, social justice and are willing to take bold steps in bringing about change in society. this internship is unpaid, but we do offer college credit/service hours.

wya is more than just a youth organization; we are a movement. if you are passionate about making a difference, advocating for the rights and well-being of young people, and looking to gain invaluable experience in youth activism and leadership, we encourage you to apply.

title: sales development intern
description:
we are hiring! the aro business unit at ingersoll rand seeks a sales development intern to support our sales enablement team in bryan ohio. this hands-on summer internship invites you to dive into the heart of our sales enablement team and immerse yourself in the dynamic world of sales.

job summary

the sales development intern will play a pivotal role in lead qualification, ensuring our sales team is equipped with a robust pipeline of opportunities to grow the business. this isn't just about making calls; it's about shaping strategies, refining tools, and deploying resources to maximize sales productivity and effectiveness.

throughout this internship, you'll gain invaluable skills in sales excellence; from lead qualification techniques to process optimization and collaborative stakeholder engagement. this experience will provide developing and implementing strategies that drive impactful business outcomes for aro.

join us and empower our sales force for success while gaining hands-on experience and shaping the future of sales enablement at aro!

key position responsibilities

lead management: the process of categorizing and engaging with inbound leads promptly and efficiently based on predefined criteria.

classify inbound leads based on needs, budget, and decision-making capabilities.ensure prompt engagement by working through the inbound lead queue via phone calls and emails within a 4-hour timeframe.efficiently route leads to sales partners based on region, product, and other qualifying attributes.

sales process optimization: the analysis and enhancement of sales procedures to improve efficiency and effectiveness.

analyze sales processes to identify areas for improvement.collaborate with team to implement enhancements and streamline workflows, ultimately boosting operational efficiency.

content development: creation of engaging resources aligned with brand messaging and sales objectives.

partner with the team to create compelling sales collateral, presentations, and training materials.ensure all content aligns with brand messaging and sales objectives.

standard work: establishment and documentation of consistent procedures to ensure efficiency and effectiveness in lead management and other tasks.

establish, update and document lead management standard work to ensure consistency and efficiency.assist in training on processes, tools, and templates.develop scripting for frequently asked questions.

documentation: organizing and maintaining accurate records of lead status and actions taken for reference and follow-up.

organize, maintain, and update lead status in the lead generation system.review lead status and follow up with sales on leads not acted on according to process metrics and detractor ratings.

reporting and analytics: tracking, analyzing, and reporting on key performance indicators to identify trends and inform strategic decision-making.

assist in tracking and analyzing key performance indicators/metrics (kpi’s).generate reports/dashboards to provide insights into sales effectiveness, identifying areas for growth.collaborate with stakeholders to determine countermeasures for unfavorable metrics.

work arrangement and travel requirements

this is an onsite internship located at the aro office in bryan oh. 

key qualifications

undergraduate student, at least sophomore level, currently pursuing a bachelor’s degree in business, communications, marketing, or a related field at ability to work at least 20 hours per week, up to 40 hours per week. strong communication skills both written and on phone.high level of organization and time management skillslistens and responds in professional manner.3.0 cumulative gpa

title: sales development internship - nationwide (summer '24)
description:
ingersoll rand is committed to achieving workforce diversity reflective of our communities. we are an equal opportunity employer. all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to age, ancestry, color, family or medical care leave, gender identity or expression, genetic information, marital status, medical condition, national origin, physical or mental disability, political affiliation, protected veteran status, race, religion, sex (including pregnancy), sexual orientation, or any other characteristic protected by applicable laws, regulations and ordinances.

sales development internship job description

ingersoll rand's industrial sales development internship is a 12 week program designed to prepare students for a career in technical sales. this internship is offered to college students pursuing a bachelor’s or master’s degree in engineering, with a 2.9 gpa or higher.

as a participant, your internship will be geared toward building your skills and knowledge as a sales engineer, and developing your ability to consult, problem-solve and design solutions for a wide variety of our industrial customers.

the internship is based at one of our 36 customer center locations nationwide. during the summer you will work under the guidance of an area sales leader to gain hands-on experience and build customer relationship skills as you job-shadow experienced sales engineers, call on customers and participate in selling our compressed air products and services.

the summer internship ends by presenting to a leadership team your success stories and delivery towards revenue generating activities. interns are provided feedback on their performance and sales aptitude at the conclusion of the summer.

title: service technician
description:
immediately hiring: service technician

location: east syracuse, ny

pay range: $25-35/hr travel is within 3 hour radius of territory

this job provides a company vehicle and company card for travel expenses** 

the role

the service technician for ingersoll rand&aposs industrial technologies and services division serves customers by installing, troubleshooting, repairing and maintaining all makes of rotary screw and reciprocating and/or centac and ecc air compressors, along with additional compressed air system components. you will use precision measuring tools for diagnostic trouble shooting of compressed air equipment. in addition, you need to have knowledge of all operations and parts manuals associated with all equipment.

job responsibilities

effectively communicates verbally and in writing with customers and co-workers. provides technical assistance on equipment pre-commissioning and start-ups, installations and repairs of compressed air systems. including “hands-on” work to assure equipment operates to customer’s requirement with limited guidance from the master technician. conducts safety assessments of a continuously changing working environment. able to read wiring schematics and diagrams for all equipment. serves as technical support for customers and customer service managers documents service and installation activities by completing work orders for each job, maintenance checklist and daily time sheets. increases ingersoll rand customers awareness of how ingersoll rand products, services and solutions; such as package care, preventative maintenance, diagnostics, audits and leak detection can benefit their operations. maintains rapport with customers by addressing complaints, identifying solutions, suggesting improved methods and techniques and recommending system improvements. explains practices, procedures, and policies to reach agreement with suppliers and others outside of the job area. provides assistance and training to apprentices and field service technicians. ensures proper operation of compressed air equipment by completing preventative maintenance requirements and 47 point inspections on a wide variety of compressors, refrigerated dryers, desiccant dryers and additional compressed air components. works with service coordinator on ordering and gathering parts and supplies necessary to complete installations and repairs. advanced network systems installations (immc/asc, x series automation) basic vibration troubleshooting basic air quality (system compressor/dryer – air treatment) 

candidate qualifications

desired applicants will have 2-5 years’ experience of troubleshooting industrial/commercial mechanical systems. high school diploma or equivalent required desired degree program in electrical/electronics technology or hvac with advanced knowledge of some or all of the following: ac/dc circuits, wiring diagrams/schematics, motors/controls, plcs, hvac electricity, controls, refrigeration systems and/or refrigerant certification. candidates who possess this knowledge or skills but have not completed a formal program may be considered. operating knowledge of microsoft office software and other basic computer usage. proficiency with hand held computer (i.e. smartphone, ipad) and network bases advanced verbal and written communication skills advanced customer service skills including conflict management and opportunity development/consulting use of advanced troubleshooting tools candidates must provide their own tools attend required ingersoll rand training must possess a valid driver&aposs license for a minimum of 12 months, with no major or frequent traffic violations including, but not limited to: dui in the previous 5 years, hit & run, license suspension, reckless/careless driving or multiple smaller infractions or preventable collisions in the previous 3 years. epa 608/f-gas 303/2008/ce/ishrae certification preferred (refrigeration experience/certification a plus). this position requires working in diverse conditions including but not limited to mechanical equipment rooms with exposure to noisy operating equipment, exposure to extreme weather conditions, precarious places, and risk of electrical shock (high voltage) with nfpa or equivalent standards. this position is highly physical and requires regular use of hands, fingers, walking, stooping, and kneeling. it requires employees to regularly lift and or move weight limits in line with the safety policy. 

we are committed to helping you reach your professional, personal and financial goals. we offer competitive compensation that aligns with our business strategies and comprehensive benefits to help you live your healthiest. we are committed to building an inclusive and diverse culture that engages as well as values the different backgrounds and experiences of our employee, which, in turn, spurs innovation, generates creative solutions and enhances our customer relations.?

if you share our passion for inspiring progress—for bringing about bold shifts in how people, economies and societies operate—then you belong with ingersoll rand. progress begins with you.

title: diesel fitter - multiple locations
description:
lots of training and fantastic career progression opportunitiesjob security with an established businessstrong sense of community and mateship within the team

about the position

we are currently seeking applications for a experienced diesel mechanics or light vehicle motor mechanics to join our mobile teams located in the herbert region, burdekin region, proserpine and mackay region. this is a full-time permanent position, and the successful applicants will be responsible for safely and effectively providing maintenance of locomotive fleet and mobile plant to maximise locomotive and plant availability. motor mechanics who have had previous experience with diesel engines should apply and in house training will be provided to suitable applicants if they are successful.

the salary offered will be commensurate with individual skills and experience. the indicative salary range is between $70,000 (tl10) and $77,000 (tl08) annually for day work (during the maintenance season) and moves to shift work with shift penalties in the production season. penalties are paid on any time done over 38 hours!

main duties include but are not limited to:

servicing and tuning of engines and the diagnosis/maintenance/repair of locomotive systems (transmissions, drives lubrication, fuel, braking, induction/exhaust and suspension)mtu series 200/60 diesel engines, mercedes 906,926, 502 diesel engines repairs and servicingallisson 3000 and 4000 automatic transmission repairs and servicing, repairs and servicing of renk magg drive unitsconstructing and commissioning of capital-built locos and brake vansrepairs and servicing on various mobile plant, franna cranes, forklifts, trucks, loaders, bobcats, tractors etc.assisting in the identification and scoping of work required for the maintenance of locos and mobile planttroubleshooting and fault reporting of equipment during breakdownscontributing to the quality control techniques and continuous improvement of work methodsensuring work orders and related permits are completed to a standardmaintaining safe work practicesproviding trade guidance to fellow team members including apprentices

additional benefits for working with wilmar sugar include:

supported career progression whether it is down the technical stream or onwards to leadership (internships, cadetships, internal courses, and university education)employees who live in our mill/farm/distillery regions in queensland may be eligible to claim remote area tax benefits via maxxia. employees may be able to achieve tax benefits on rent, and on housing purchase and interest payments.additional recreational leave accrualemployee wellbeing programs such as free flu vaccinations and access to our eap providerdiscounts with our corporate partners such as blackwood's, specsavers, medibank private, kathmandu and many more 

essential criteria:

certificate iii in mechanical engineering (diesel fitter, motor mechanic) or certificate iii light vehicle mechanical technology (we will upskill you to be a diesel fitter!)previous heavy industrial work history highly regarded

other desirable skills - but if you don't have them, we will train you over time!

working at heights, hydraulics, pneumatics, dogman licence, forklift, overhead crane ticket, welding skills, ulv (24v) electrical exposure

how to apply

confidential applications should be submitted online by 8.00am 13 may 2024 by hitting the "apply" button. for further information regarding the position please contact shanna southern, people & culture on 07 4945 9773. the successful candidate will be required to complete a pre-employment medical including drug & alcohol screening.

about wilmar sugar and renewables

with a history of over 160 years in the australian and new zealand sugar industries, wilmar sugar australia operations span the value chain from sugar cane growing, sugar manufacturing, refining, exporting, and marketing, to the production of renewable electricity, ethanol, and sustainable agricultural products.

wilmar manufactures over half of australia's raw sugar, and each year exports enough electricity to power more than 68,000 australian homes for 12 months. as well as supplying ethanol (ethyl alcohol) to the growing fuel market in australia, wilmar also supplies a significant market share to various industries including the pharmaceutical, printing, cleaning, winery, aerosol, animal health and food and beverage industries.

business unit non seasonal

responsibilities:• team with one senior team member as the mentor to get familiar with the overall missionof ds team and be a team player to work on one or more ongoing projects.• collaborate on the development and implementation of machine learning models usingstate-of-the-art techniques.• conduct experiments to optimize model hyperparameters to improve performance.• evaluate model performance using proper metrics and iterate the process as necessary.
skills and qualifications:• currently pursuing or recently graduated with an advanced degree in computer science,data science, statistics, or a related quantitative field• solid understanding of basic machine learning concepts and techniques.• familiar with data processing such as data cleansing, transformation, and featureengineering.• strong problem solving and attention to detail.• prior experience with popular machine learning libraries is a plus.

title: human resources intern
description:
while primarily a human resources internship, this is a shared internship with finance. this intern will assist the human resources manager in several facets of the human resources field, as well as assisting the finance department with ap/ar from time to time. this is a part-time, paid internship, averaging about 20 hours/wk. this position has the potential to change from a seasonal internship to a continued part-time job. possible areas covered in this internship include:
• onboarding – starting with posting jobs and reviewing applications and going through the employee’s first week.• offboarding – reviewing the disciplinary process, handling documentation, sending termination notices, and responding to unemployment claims.• data management – reviewing employee files, gathering missing information, handling i-9 forms, and much more.• compliance – assist with dot driver files to ensure compliance with the fmcsa, review company handbooks to ensure compliance with federal, state, and local laws, and prepare documentation for eeo filings• payroll – process payroll, enter payroll into accounting software, allocating payroll to correct job costing classes, monthly worker’s comp entries/payments, acquiring tax ids for new jurisdictions as needed, etc.• benefits – help new employees get enrolled in benefit plans, reconcile monthly bills with employee deductions, answer employee questions regarding employee benefits• compensation – help in reviewing/revising job descriptions, assist with compensation analysis, use comp analysis to set salary bands, ensure we’re meeting all required pay transparency requirements• misc. – assist with scanning employee documents into their e-file to reduce paper storage, work on employee recognition program, other general office duties• ap/ar – assist finance with accounts payable and accounts receivable
pcci group started as perley cable construction in 1981 providing construction services to telecommunications companies across the us. in 1989 the owner added to his business, creating a local telecom company called tel-star communications (then called tel-star cablevision). tel-star is a local telecom company providing cable, internet, and phone services to underserved rural areas. then, in 1993, we added prodesign engineering to our group. prodesign maps and designs telecom networks, not only for our own telecom companies, but for different telecom providers across the united states. finally, in 2019, we added i5 fiber – a local fiber optic internet provider in bonita springs, fl. perley cable construction currently has projects in galesburg and in punta gorda, fl. tel-star is in the process of upgrading our network from coaxial service to fiber optics. prodesign has had field crews in michigan, wisconsin, louisianna, texas, florida, and colorado over the last couple of years mapping telecom networks for our clients. i5 fiber is still building their network throughout bonita springs as they continue to grow.

title: business analyst intern - summer
description:
about mplc
founded in 1986 by a former vice-president of paramount pictures and mgm, mplc now has offices in 28 countries with headquarters split between the uk and usa. our uk hq is in eastbourne sussex while in the usa, our hq is in los angeles, next to many of the hollywood studios that we represent.
mplc is the market leader in non-theatrical licensing. we are on a mission to enable companies to use world-class film and television entertainment while supporting the creative industries and protecting their intellectual property.
at its simplest, companies around the world use tv and films to help their business. whether it is gyms trying to create a great environment for their customers or employers making their staff/break rooms a better place to be, hundreds of thousands of companies are showing content in public spaces. a license is required to use that content.
mplc provides that license to businesses across industries, whether it is a single restaurant or a large global corporation. we then provide royalties from the licenses to our rights holders ranging from major hollywood studios to local tv production companies.
mplc is a key player in the entertainment and intellectual property ecosystem and helps to increase content copyright awareness across all organizations.
our company is on a truly exciting journey. we’re growing fast, adapting to new technology and trends, and have ambitious plans for the future. yet, we also remember to keep it fun, down-to-earth, and globally collaborative as “who you are with” and “the process of getting there” matter just as much as destination. 😊 we would love for you to be a part of our journey and story!
about the position 
the u.s. business operations and strategy team is looking for a summer intern. this is a non-paid, academic credit internship open to both current undergraduate and graduate students. the role will not only support strategic and operational initiatives but also work in a rotational capacity supporting and learning from other business divisions (sales, marketing, legal, tech, etc.).
every day is different, with each project directly impacting and supporting the company’s growth and expansion in the u.s. market.
after this internship, you will not only gain new functional skills in a wide variety of business functions, insight into a company’s growth process and strategic decisions, but also gain strategy management consulting style problem solving, communication, and project management skills.
if the traits below sound like you, we’d love to hear from you... 
we are an equal opportunity employer and love all types of folks who enjoy and would excel in this type of role. we are especially interested if you are the following: you are inquisitive and a problem solver. if you don’t know the answer, you know how to find it.you are logical and detail oriented.you are competent with microsoft suite, especially excel and powerpoint.you have experience with data analysis using excel.you work well independently as well as in teams.you are currently an undergraduate student in your 2nd year or higher, or a graduate student.you have at least 1 year of professional experience.
we have a 2 day in office 3 day remote culture. this position is for the los angeles office. however, we welcome all great candidates even if you are remote!

title: service technician
description:
immediately hiring: service technician

location: long island, ny or edison, nj

pay range: $25-35/hr

travel is within 3 hour radius of territory

**this job provides a company vehicle and company card for travel expenses**

the role

the field service technician for ingersoll rand's industrial technologies and services division serves customers by installing, troubleshooting, repairing and maintaining all makes of rotary screw and reciprocating and/or centac and ecc air compressors, along with additional compressed air system components. you will use precision measuring tools for diagnostic trouble shooting of compressed air equipment. in addition, you need to have knowledge of all operations and parts manuals associated with all equipment.

job responsibilities

effectively communicates verbally and in writing with customers and co-workers.provides technical assistance on equipment pre-commissioning and start-ups, installations and repairs of compressed air systems. including “hands-on” work to assure equipment operates to customer’s requirement with limited guidance from the master technician.conducts safety assessments of a continuously changing working environment.able to read wiring schematics and diagrams for all equipment.serves as technical support for customers and customer service managersdocuments service and installation activities by completing work orders for each job, maintenance checklist and daily time sheets.increases ingersoll rand customers awareness of how ingersoll rand products, services and solutions; such as package care, preventative maintenance, diagnostics, audits and leak detection can benefit their operations.maintains rapport with customers by addressing complaints, identifying solutions, suggesting improved methods and techniques and recommending system improvements.explains practices, procedures, and policies to reach agreement with suppliers and others outside of the job area.provides assistance and training to apprentices and field service technicians.ensures proper operation of compressed air equipment by completing preventative maintenance requirements and 47 point inspections on a wide variety of compressors, refrigerated dryers, desiccant dryers and additional compressed air components.works with service coordinator on ordering and gathering parts and supplies necessary to complete installations and repairs.advanced network systems installations (immc/asc, x series automation)basic vibration troubleshootingbasic air quality (system compressor/dryer – air treatment)



candidate qualifications

desired applicants will have 2-5 years’ experience of troubleshooting industrial/commercial mechanical systems.high school diploma or equivalent requireddesired degree program in electrical/electronics technology or hvac with advanced knowledge of some or all of the following: ac/dc circuits, wiring diagrams/schematics, motors/controls, plcs, hvac electricity, controls, refrigeration systems and/or refrigerant certification. candidates who possess this knowledge or skills but have not completed a formal program may be considered.operating knowledge of microsoft office software and other basic computer usage. proficiency with hand held computer (i.e. smartphone, ipad) and network basesadvanced verbal and written communication skillsadvanced customer service skills including conflict management and opportunity development/consultinguse of advanced troubleshooting toolscandidates must provide their own toolsattend required ingersoll rand trainingmust possess a valid driver's license for a minimum of 12 months, with no major or frequent traffic violations including, but not limited to: dui in the previous 5 years, hit & run, license suspension, reckless/careless driving or multiple smaller infractions or preventable collisions in the previous 3 years.epa 608/f-gas 303/2008/ce/ishrae certification preferred (refrigeration experience/certification a plus).this position requires working in diverse conditions including but not limited to mechanical equipment rooms with exposure to noisy operating equipment, exposure to extreme weather conditions, precarious places, and risk of electrical shock (high voltage) with nfpa or equivalent standards.this position is highly physical and requires regular use of hands, fingers, walking, stooping, and kneeling. it requires employees to regularly lift and or move weight limits in line with the safety policy.

we are committed to helping you reach your professional, personal and financial goals. we offer competitive compensation that aligns with our business strategies and comprehensive benefits to help you live your healthiest. we are committed to building an inclusive and diverse culture that engages as well as values the different backgrounds and experiences of our employee, which, in turn, spurs innovation, generates creative solutions and enhances our customer relations.?

if you share our passion for inspiring progress—for bringing about bold shifts in how people, economies and societies operate—then you belong with ingersoll rand. progress begins with you.

description:

about us

job summary

ingersoll rand’s ir next program will provide recent college graduates with challenging assignments in a variety of finance functional areas, leadership training and valuable exposure to our businesses and our company's senior leaders. through functional rotations and experiences in multiple business locations, selected candidates will be able to build diverse skill sets and a strong foundation for a successful and fulfilling career with ingersoll rand.

these assignments and specialized development curriculum are strategically designed to ensure the candidate’s continuous learning and development. ingersoll rand’s 24-month rotational program offers candidates who have recently obtained their bachelor's degree in accounting/finance (or other related field) exposure to a variety of assignments which could include: cost accounting, inventory management, budgeting and planning, commercial finance, financial analysis, internal controls, compliance and project management, and treasury.

the objective of the ir next program is to develop and grow the participant’s financial and business acumen as well as the leadership skills necessary to prepare them for a role in the finance organization upon successful completion of the program.

basic qualifications

we are looking for performance-driven, innovative leaders who understand the importance of collaboration and customer focus.

bachelor’s degree in accounting, finance, or related field completed between may 2023 and june 2024, or 5th year master’s degree in accounting or finance, to be completed between december 2023 and june 2024related majors include: accounting, finance, economics, business administration, math, or operationshave completed, or plan to complete, accounting courses up to and including intermediate accounting (or college/university equivalent)preferred cumulative gpa of 3.0 or aboveprevious finance co-op/internship or other practical experience in the manufacturing industry preferredleadership experience in an extra-curricular activity or organizationavailable for duration of the program (24 months) with a tentative start date of august 2024sponsorship for work visas are not available for this program

travel & work arrangements/requirements

this position will be based within the us with occasional travel. must be willing to relocate to any ingersoll rand location, up to two times, over the duration of the program.

key competencies

exceptional organization, communication (verbal and written), and follow-up skills with the ability to effectively manage priorities and commitments in a fast-paced work environment strong interpersonal skills, able to follow standard processes and work autonomously while understanding when to escalate issues that require further reviewutilize financial data to diagnose business strengths and weaknesses as well as the implications for potential strategiescollaborate with others to foster effective teamwork by linking own responsibilities to the team’s goals and needs - zeal for new information, knowledge, and experiencesability to learn quickly, embrace change, and thrive in a fast-paced environmentself-starters who are results-oriented, analytical, innovative, intellectually curious and who take initiative and risksdemonstrated leadership skills such as: campus organizations, sports team captain, resident advisor, etc. or exceptional leadership of work projects

ingersoll rand offers a comprehensive benefits program that includes, but is not limited to, health and retirement benefits.

title: sales development internship - nationwide (summer '24)
description:
ingersoll rand is committed to achieving workforce diversity reflective of our communities. we are an equal opportunity employer. all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to age, ancestry, color, family or medical care leave, gender identity or expression, genetic information, marital status, medical condition, national origin, physical or mental disability, political affiliation, protected veteran status, race, religion, sex (including pregnancy), sexual orientation, or any other characteristic protected by applicable laws, regulations and ordinances.

sales development internship job description

ingersoll rand's industrial sales development internship is a 12 week program designed to prepare students for a career in technical sales. this internship is offered to college students pursuing a bachelor’s or master’s degree in engineering, with a 2.9 gpa or higher.

as a participant, your internship will be geared toward building your skills and knowledge as a sales engineer, and developing your ability to consult, problem-solve and design solutions for a wide variety of our industrial customers.

the internship is based at one of our 36 customer center locations nationwide. during the summer you will work under the guidance of an area sales leader to gain hands-on experience and build customer relationship skills as you job-shadow experienced sales engineers, call on customers and participate in selling our compressed air products and services.

the summer internship ends by presenting to a leadership team your success stories and delivery towards revenue generating activities. interns are provided feedback on their performance and sales aptitude at the conclusion of the summer.

these opportunities are available nationwide. a specific location will be discussed at a later time.

additional requirements

pursuing a bachelor's degree in engineering is requiredto qualify for consideration, you should have a strong technical aptitude and a solutions-selling mindset; solid teamwork skills; excellent written, verbal and presentation communication skills; and an ability to relocate geographically.previous internship and leadership experiences are preferred but not required.

starting pay is $22-$28 per hour but may be adjusted based on qualifications and experience. this is a full-time, hourly position.

mergers & acquisitionssell-side advisorybuy-side advisorystrategic advisorycapital raisingfairness opinions and valuationsprivate placements

the student radiologic technologist i assists in meeting the needs of patients, team members, clinicians and departmental operations by providing clerical, technical and clinical operational support. under the direct / indirect supervision of a registered radiologic technologist during clinical education, the student radiologic technologist i assists physicians in the performance of diagnostic and / or therapeutic procedures by preparing the patient and operating the x-ray equipment in order to obtain diagnostic images.

responsibilities

it is the responsibility of every novant health team member to deliver the most remarkable patient experience in every dimension, every time.

title: international apprentice - spring 2025
description:
job description

get involved!

are you getting ready to graduate or are you a recent college graduate seeking an opportunity to serve internationally? do you desire to leverage your skills to help others and proclaim the gospel? if you are searching for a career with impact this is a unique opportunity to join the future global samaritan's purse team through the samaritan's purse apprenticeship program! we are now accepting applications for international apprentice - spring 2025! the start date for this next cohort will be mid-january, 2025.

a day in the life of an international apprentice

samaritan's purse exists to provide spiritual and physical aid to hurting people around the world in jesus' name. we are searching for talented, high-capacity individuals who want to start a career with samaritan's purse and have a sense of god's calling on their lives to ministry and to serve internationally.

the samaritan's purse apprentice program is a strategic initiative designed to prepare and train the next generation of samaritan's purse's global workforce. the program will equip individuals with the necessary skills to serve in full-time roles in one of our international field offices. international apprentice positions will be up to two years in length and include salary and benefits. the apprenticeship aims to equip individuals for full-time work internationally by training and developing them in the needed competencies. there are four primary tracks in which apprentices are typically placed: program management, operations, finance, and security.

while full-time jobs are not guaranteed at the end of the two-year contract, the majority of those who have joined the program have been able to move into full-time employment with samaritan's purse. the international apprentice position supports various samaritan's purse field offices around the globe during the course of the program.

an ideal candidate for an international apprentice will have

 a sense of calling to the ministry and long-term interest in the work of samaritan's purse  an ability to problem solve and drive results  a flexible attitude  humility to do whatever task is required; willing to learn  ability to work under pressure and perform well in high-demand, high-volume work environments 

to learn more about the apprentice program and hear about the program from current apprentices click .

international apprentices must be able to commit to serving with samaritans purse for a minimum of 24 months.

learn more about .

qualifications & experience

 maintain a personal, active relationship with jesus christ and is a consistent witness for jesus christ  a bachelor's or master's degree required (degrees in the areas of health, finance/accounting, engineering, business, supply chain management, program management, operations, international relations preferred)  analytical, strategic thinking, and demonstrated leadership skills  adaptable to working in a variety of international contexts  ability to develop and implement plans and solve problems independently  demonstrate strong organizational, communication (written and verbal), and networking skills  attend daily morning devotions and participate in prayer support for the ministry, donors, and volunteers  proficiency in a foreign language, (especially french, spanish, or arabic), is a plus  prior international experience is strongly preferred  follow the policies and procedures of samaritan's purse as set forth in the policy manual  12 credit hours of college-level biblical studies 

job location:

international apprentice positions serve the first 4-5 months at our ihq in boone and north wilkesboro, nc, for the initial training phases and then the remainder of their apprenticeship in one or more of our international field offices.

type: full-time, 2-year contract

language requirement: english required

travel expectations:  the position requires frequent international travel, including extended placements in samaritan's purse international field offices.

international employee benefits

employees who choose to work internationally get to take advantage of the following benefits:

 international medical, prescription, dental & vision insurance  paid family medical leave  retirement savings plan  paid holidays  12 vacation/personal days  10 sick days  when at ihq, on-site medical clinic 

samaritan's purse mission statement

samaritan's purse is a nondenominational evangelical christian organization providing spiritual and physical aid to hurting people around the world. since 1970, samaritan's purse has helped meet needs of people who are victims of war, poverty, natural disasters, disease, and famine with the purpose of sharing god's love through his son, jesus christ. the organization serves the church worldwide to promote the gospel of the lord jesus christ.

samaritan's purse statement of faith

 we believe the bible to be the inspired, the only infallible, authoritative word of god. (1 thessalonians 2:13; 2 timothy 3:15-17)  we believe that there is one god, eternally existent in three persons: father, son, and holy spirit. (matthew 28:19; john 10:30; ephesians 4:4-6)  we believe in the deity of the lord jesus christ, in his virgin birth, in his sinless life, in his miracles, in his vicarious and atoning death through his shed blood on the cross, in his bodily resurrection, in his ascension to the right hand of the father, and in his personal return in power and glory. (matthew 1:23; john 1:1-4 and 1:29; acts 1:11 and 2:22-24; romans 8:34; 1 corinthians 15:3-4; 2 corinthians 5:21; philippians 2:5-11; hebrews 1:1-4 and 4:15)  we believe that all men everywhere are lost and face the judgment of god, that jesus christ is the only way of salvation, and that for the salvation of lost and sinful man, repentance of sin and faith in jesus christ results in regeneration by the holy spirit. (luke 24:46-47; john 14:6; acts 4:12; romans 3:23; 2 corinthians 5:10-11; ephesians 1:7 and 2:8-9; titus 3:4-7)  we believe in the present ministry of the holy spirit, whose indwelling enables the christian to live a godly life. (john 3:5-8; acts 1:8 and 4:31; romans 8:9; 1 corinthians 2:14; galatians 5:16-18; ephesians 6:12; colossians 2:6-10)  we believe in the resurrection of both the saved and the lost; the saved unto the resurrection of eternal life and the lost unto the resurrection of damnation and eternal punishment. (1 corinthians 15:51-57; revelation 20:11-15)  we believe in the spiritual unity of believers in the lord jesus christ and that all true believers are members of his body, the church. (1 corinthians 12:12, 27; ephesians 1:22-23)  we believe that the ministry of evangelism and discipleship is a responsibility of all followers of jesus christ. (matthew 28:18-20; acts 1:8; romans 10:9-15; 1 peter 3:15)  we believe gods plan for human sexuality is to be expressed only within the context of marriage, that god created man and woman as unique biological persons made to complete each other. god instituted monogamous marriage between male and female as the foundation of the family and the basic structure of human society. for this reason, we believe that marriage is exclusively the union of one genetic male and one genetic female. (genesis 2:24; matthew 19:5-6; mark 10:6-9; romans 1:26-27; 1 corinthians 6:9)  we believe that we must dedicate ourselves to prayer, to the service of our lord, to his authority over our lives, and to the ministry of evangelism. (matthew 9:35-38; 22:37-39, and 28:18-20; acts 1:8; romans 10:9-15 and 12:20-21; galatians 6:10; colossians 2:6-10; 1 peter 3:15)  we believe that human life is sacred from conception to its natural end; and that we must have concern for the physical and spiritual needs of our fellowmen. (psalm 139:13; isaiah 49:1; jeremiah 1:5; matthew 22:37-39; romans 12:20-21; galatians 6:10)

title: stem summer camp instructor
description:
job description: we seek enthusiastic summer camp instructors for our 2024 camps. as a camp instructor, you will facilitate learning and fun in a dynamic, interactive environment. you will be responsible for assisting in the delivery of our curricula in scratch, python, and engineering principles to a classroom of students. each camp will have at least two instructors for at most 15 students. 
duration: summer 2024 (approximately june 3rd, 2024 through august 2nd, 2024)
schedule: roughly 8:30 am-4:30 pm monday through friday
about bx coding: bx coding is a mission-driven computer science education nonprofit dedicated to educating underserved k-12 students in the stem fields. through our outreach programs, summer camps, and cutting-edge computer science pedagogy, we aim to inspire and empower the next generation of stem leaders.
specific skills and minimum requirements:a passion for stem education.a desire to work with students ages 7-13.currently pursuing or have completed a degree in education (preferred), computer science, engineering, or a related field.excellent communication skills, including the ability to simplify complex concepts and adjust teaching methods to fit various learning styles.a commitment to bx coding’s mission and values, including fostering inclusivity and accessibility in stem education.
preferred qualifications:previous experience as a camp counselor or instructor.previous experience teaching stem at a k-12 level.
expected responsibilities:deliver lessons in scratch, python, and hands-on engineering alongside a lead instructor.facilitate a supportive and energetic learning environment that encourages student participation and creativity.provide personalized attention and support to students with diverse learning needs and experiences.manage classroom dynamics and ensure a safe and respectful environment for all students.participate in paid computer science instruction and classroom management training prior to the start of camp. complete cpr, first aid, and aed certification programs.instructors will be background-checked and fingerprinted before working with children.
compensation: $20/hour
application timeline: bx coding will review, interview, and select candidates on a rolling basis. bx coding is an equal-opportunity employer committed to building a diverse set of instructors and strongly encourages applications from individuals from underrepresented groups.

title: athletic trainer
description:
are you passionate about sports performance and looking to gain hands-on experience in the field? join all-en sports performance for our summer 2024 all-enternship program in houston, tx.
as an intern, you will have the opportunity to work directly with our team of certified trainers and elite athletes, including pro athletes like tank dell, dalvin cook, and james harden. you will assist in developing and implementing personalized training programs, conducting performance assessments, and tracking athlete progress. this internship will provide you with valuable experience in sports science, strength and conditioning, and injury prevention.
this internship is for college credit only and requires a commitment of at least 15 hours per week. ideal candidates will have a background in exercise science, kinesiology, or a related field, as well as a strong passion for sports and performance training. don't miss this opportunity to learn from the best in the industry and take your career to the next level with all-en sports performance. apply by 5/17/24 to kickstart your career in the industry! questions:katie@allensportsmanagement.com

title: renewable energy engineering & construction internship
description:
the renewable energy intern will join our team of professionals in our mc 2 renewable energy services division. this team is responsible for technical review and project execution of new renewable energy projects as well as monitoring and maintaining our existing client’s fleet of solar and battery storage installations.

pay range: $18 per hour. 40 hours per week.

interns are considered valuable members of our team, and therefore earn benefits including:

 real world experience  competitive salaries  consideration for full-time employment when you graduate 

responsibilities

 research new trends in technology, materials, manufacturing, construction, and supply chain.  participate in diligence of new deals, which includes monitoring activity for construction projects, technology reviews, etc.  support our renewable projects by aiding in data analysis, job site inspections and reporting.  assist with the resolution of technical issues within the company’s portfolio of energy projects including reviewing root cause analysis, review of incident reports, mining operational data, developing project site and maintenance reports, supporting project management, etc. 

through this position, candidates will be exposed to solar and energy storage projects. candidates will also be directly exposed to operations, equipment manufacturing, construction, project management, and site maintenance. candidates will see infrastructure projects developed, selected, financed, constructed, operated and asset managed.

qualifications

 pursuing a bachelor's degree or equivalent in engineering  demonstrates interest in and familiarity with energy industry and technologies  curiosity with interconnection between energy, politics & financial markets  creativity, enthusiasm, and drive  prior internships / working experience  high self-confidence  excellent interpersonal skills  proactive, takes initiative  must have and maintain a clean driving record  ability to travel as needed 

this job description is not intended to be all-inclusive. your supervisor may request and assign similar duties.

resumes that are mailed, emailed or hand-delivered to milton cat or any employee will not be considered. you must apply online. a background check and drug test are required as part of our pre-employment process.

milton cat is an equal opportunity employer that values the strength diversity brings to the workplace. we encourage applications from individuals with disabilities, minorities, veterans, and women. applicants receive fair and impartial consideration without regard to race, sex, color, religion, national origin, age, sexual orientation, gender identity, handicap, disability, veteran status, genetic data, or other legally protected status. we base all employment decisions to further this principle of equal employment opportunity. vevraa federal contractor. eeo/aa employer. milton cat is a drug-free workplace.

apex strives to excel in every phase of project realization, from origination and financing to construction and asset management. our mission-driven team seeks determined, resolute individuals who strive to innovate and go above the call of duty, and who will relentlessly do their part to help accelerate the shift to america’s clean energy future.
