razorfish is an equal opportunity employer.

razorfish will provide accommodations to applicants needing accommodations to complete the application process.

compensation range: $20 -$23/hr. this is the pay range the company believes it will pay for this position at the time of this posting. consistent with applicable law, compensation will be determined based on the skills, qualifications, and experience of the applicant along with the requirements of the position, and the company reserves the right to modify this pay range at any time. this role may be eligible to participate in our freelancer/temporary employee medical plan through a third-party benefits administration system once certain criteria have been met. this role may also qualify for participation in our 401(k) plan after eligibility criteria have been met.

the company anticipates the application deadline for this job posting will be 03/7/2024.

title: communications and design
description:
the county commissioners association of ohio (ccao) is a trade association that represents all 88 of ohio’s counties. ccao advances effective county government for ohio through legislative advocacy, education and training, technical assistance and research, quality enterprise service programs, and greater citizen awareness and understanding of county government. ccao is seeking a qualified candidate for a summer communications and design internship position. applications will be accepted until the position is filled. ccao is seeking an intern majoring in communications, graphic design, marketing or a related field with an interest in local government and at least two years of college experience. the intern will work 20 - 25 hours per week between mid-may and mid-august. the intern will work closely with ccao’s communications coordinator and assist with projects including publication layout and design, website updates, creation of graphics for social media and web use, storytelling through written articles and press releases, and assistance with photography and videography. the intern may work on projects for ccao, the county risk sharing authority (corsa), the county employee benefit consortium (cebco), the ccao service corporation and the ccao research & education foundation. duties and responsibilitiesassist with publication design and layout on both short and long-form materials such as annual reports, event programs, and flyerscreate graphics for use on the web and social mediacomplete website updates as neededoccasional travel to counties across ohiowrite articles for ccao’s online magazine, county leader; gather content by interviewing ccao membersoccasional assistance with photography and video production education/experience requirementsminimum 2 years of college experience or courseworkproficiency in adobe creative suite, primarily indesign, illustrator and photoshopproficiency in microsoft office, primarily word, powerpoint and excelrelevant coursework in graphic design, communications, media relations or related fields
required application materialsresumecover letterdigital design portfoliocontact information for three references, including at least one academic reference send all materials to jessica newbacher, ccao communications coordinator at jnewbacher@ccao.org. compensationthis is an hourly position with an estimated rate of $20 / hour, depending on experience and qualifications. the internship is not a permanent position and is not eligible for retirement, health, or other benefits.

title: investment banking analyst
description:
corporate profile  ziegler is a privately held investment bank, capital markets and proprietary investments firm. specializing in the healthcare, senior living and education sectors, as well as general municipal and structured finance, enables us to generate a positive impact on the communities we serve. headquartered in chicago with regional and branch offices throughout the u.s., ziegler provides its clients with capital raising, strategic advisory services, equity and fixed income sales & trading and research. 
business unit ziegler's healthcare m&a investment banking team is focused on delivering best-in-class advisory and financing solutions for companies and organizations across the healthcare industry. in our core practice areas of healthcare services, information technology, hospitals and senior living, ziegler is one of the most active m&a firms offering innovative sell-side, buy-side, recapitalization/restructuring, equity private placement and strategic partnering services. 
position overview the investment banking analyst will be responsible for leading the analytical work in support of new business development and transaction execution, as well as assisting senior bankers in the development and delivery of value-added investment banking products and services to clients and prospects. the analyst should expect a great deal of client exposure and will develop skill sets in the fundamentals of valuation, business strategy and client communication. ziegler is interested in candidates located in, or who would be willing to relocate to the nashville area. the analyst will be responsible for:performing valuation analysis and financial modeling for prospective and live transactions;assisting in the development and production of proposals and presentations to clients and prospects;interacting with members of corporate management teams and financial sponsors;executing detailed tasks involved in all phases of m&a and private placement engagements;serving as lead role in providing financial, business and market analysis with a special emphasis on reformatting financial statements and benchmarking key performance metrics;providing industry and business research;possessing an understanding of the firm's competition in its respective areas of expertise and can communicate what sets ziegler apart from that competition;being a resounding voice of ziegler corporate finance both inside and outside the firm;building a personal brand on ziegler’s healthcare corporate finance team, a person who will intuitively serve clients in an extraordinary way; andother responsibilities and projects as assigned.
professional qualificationsthe successful candidate possesses the following qualities: ba/bs with demonstrated coursework in finance, economics, accounting, mathematics or related subjects (or analytical work experience in lieu of such coursework);experience in investment banking/m&a ideal (internship ideal);strong quantitative and analytical skills including experience in valuation, financial modeling and analysis. financial modeling skills and business strategy execution skills are a must;high level of accounting knowledge and proficiency around 3-statement financial analysis;significant transaction experience;strong confidence in microsoft excel and powerpoint capabilities;outstanding interpersonal, verbal and written communication skills;strong project management skills and attention to detail; andflexibility in working extended hours and high travel demands.
personal qualifications strong interpersonal, verbal, and written communication skills;solid organizational and time management skills;demonstratable self-starter and ability to complete and start new tasks without daily supervision;a career-motivated professional who is self-confident, energetic, and possesses a superb work ethic;ability to thrive in a fast-paced environment and to manage multiple concurrent assignments;ability to quickly learn new concepts and activities;ability to work independently as well as within a team; andflexibility to travel, sometimes with little advance notice
the applicable base salary range for this role is $95,000 to $105,000.
the base pay offered will be determined on factors such as experience, skills, training, location, license, and education. in addition to the base salary, this position is eligible for a performance-based bonus. competitive benefits package offered to full-time associates.
ziegler is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace dedicated to pursuing and hiring a diverse workforce.

title: child and family associate therapist
description:
paid associate position in san francisco- hiring may 2024! 
are you an amft that loves working with children? join our team of dedicated play therapists in a private practice setting! sf bay play therapy family counseling center is hiring one associate mft to see clients out of our san francisco location. this is not a “get your kid hours and get out” position! it’s more of an “omg i found other play therapists who get the struggle and passion to work with kids and can support me!!” position. we are a small close-knit team and work with clinicians through and beyond licensure.
sf bay play therapy family counseling center was created by karen wolfe, lmft with the intent to provide caring, in-depth play therapy services and education for families with young children in san francisco and the east bay. we know that the drive to support children and families through therapy in our play and emotion-averse culture is taxing so we created this group to train and support one another. the work of caregiving children was never meant to be done alone!
as an employer and supervisors we value connection, authenticity, equity, and continued growth as therapists, humans, and playing people!
for more information and to apply visit:https://www.sfbayplaytherapy.com/job-opportunities.htmlwe typically hire a few times every year for positions in the east bay and san franciscomore about the job and perks
start date: may 2024
work schedule: wednesday and friday for in-person clients in sf. group supervision thursday in oakland. individual supervision(tbd). telehealth clients 2 days of your choosing. this is a full time position, though it does take time to fill a caseload.
office locationssan francisco (for in-person clients) : 2354 post st, san francisco 94115oakland (for group supervision): 3655 grand ave, oakland, ca 94610orinda (for gatherings): greenwood court, orinda, ca 94563
small group of dedicated play therapists. we strive to create a family feel!large fully stocked play therapy offices flexible telehealth hours in addition to your dedicated in-person hoursconnect to a team of other play therapists as well as our extended network to collaborate and share the joys and challenges of doing play therapy with kids! targeted tools for including parents in therapy! deep training in hand in hand parenting through an annual 8-week intensive taught by karen wolfe, lmft as well as training throughout the year to integrate into practiceannual training in treating internalized oppression by heidi stauber, lmftquarterly trainings by specialists in topics of interest to the teamweekly group and individual supervision weekly training hourbi-weekly drop in consult hour with karen wolfe, lmftsemi-annual team outings and lunch birthday gatheringsaccess to extensive training library
compensation$60 per clinical hour to start (20 hrs/wk) **it can take anywhere from 3-6 months to build up to 20 clinical hours weekly**
$20 per admin/supervision/training hour (8 hrs/wk)
job type: full time
qualifications to apply:master’s degree active amft license in caminimum of one school year experience doing play therapy with children age 5-12 (no exceptions. work with children beyond this preferred)able to see clients in person in san francisco and attend group supervision in oakland every other week (every other week is virtual) as per available office days (see below) as well as over telehealthpersonable, energetic and compassionate personality

availability needed: 28 hours per week
20 clinical hours/week:associates will choose between the following office days, with a minimum of 5 clients per office day. all associates must see clients on at least one weekend day.
san francisco: wednesdays and fridays2 days telehealth at any time of your choosing during the week8 administrative/supervisory hours per week:
supervision: 1 hour individual, 2 hours group per week (every other week in oakland)business/training hour: 1 hour per week4 admin hours/wk: calls/emails to clients (1 hr per 5 clinical hrs)

compensation$60 per clinical hour to start$20 per admin hour (1 admin hour per 5 clinical hours. supervision and business meeting paid at admin rate)tech stipend: $15/moopportunity to contribute to a retirement plan and 2% total wage matching from employersick time accrual (1 hour per 30 hours worked)malpractice insurance coveragebuilt-in pipeline of child clientsopportunity to focus on clients without marketing, creating forms, online presence, etc

applicant requirements:must have a minimum of one school year's experience doing play therapy with children ages 5-10must have a passion for working with children and families (it is unlikely you will see adult individuals in this practice so you must love play therapy with kids!)must have interest and stamina to have 20 clinical hours with children/families per weekmust maintain a minimum of 5 clients per office day and 10 telehealth sessions per week must be able to attend group supervision/training on thursdays in 11am-2pm (every other week in oakland or orinda, the other weeks via zoom)must be able to attend individual supervision scheduled with supervisor on a monday-friday as required by the bbsmust have an active amft number in good standing to applymust be able to onboard a minimum of 2 clients per week until caseload is fullwe ask for an intention to stay with our group for a minimum of 1 year (we ideally like to work with clinicians 2+ years). we offer licensed positions for those who would like to stay on
job duties:contact new client inquiries within 24 hours of receipt. schedule an intro call with them within one week of contact.promptly create and keep all billing and client documentation up to date, accurate and complete in a manner that meets the industry regulations including progress notes, consent forms, good faith estimates, etc.provide therapeutic treatment services and/or interventions such as, but not limited to; skills-training, psychoeducation, psychotherapy (individual, family, and/or group), crisis services, linkages, and referrals.attend weekly group supervision/training and business meeting thursdays 11am-2pm. every other week is virtual, and the other weeks are in oakland or orinda.must weekly individual or triadic supervision (triadic begins after 6+ months when you and your supervisor agree)abide by all laws, rules, regulations, and code of ethics as set forth by the bbs, camft, and other associated regulating bodies.track hours using payroll system and submit payroll information by the 2nd of every month


you’ll be a good fit if you possess the following:
master's degree in psychology, social work, or marriage and family counseling (required)a minimum of one year experience working with school-age children and families providing individual and family play therapyprefer if you have experience working with children with learning differences such as sensory processing disorder, adhd, and high functioning autism, or at least a desire to learnstrong ability to personally relate one-on-one with each client and build solid relationshipsstrong interest in working with supervisors to vulnerably self-reflect and grow as a person and therapistyou have a passion for supporting and working with children willing to work after school hours and eveningsability to commit to working with a family on a continuous basis for 3 to 6 months at a minimum (we do long term person-centered therapy).ability to manage client-induced anxiety and set boundaries with intrusive clients in a loving and healthy mannerability to tolerate the stress of being a new therapist and ask for help during designated supervision timesbonus if you have experience and interest in working with couples!
if you are interested fill out the screening questions and email them along with your cover letter and resume to sfbayplaytherapy@gmail.com with the title "associateship for sf bay play therapy family counseling center”
screening questions1. why do you want to join a group private practice focusing on doing play therapy with children ages 5-10 at this time?
2. what is your experience with using child-centered play therapy with children ages 5-10?
3. what is your experience with therapist-directed therapies (both expressive arts and cbt) with children ages 5-12?
4. are you interested in being part of a community of close-knit play therapists and training in sand tray, hand in hand parenting, and child-centered play therapy?
5. does the schedule work for you? saturday and/or sunday and monday for in-person clients. group supervision thursday. individual supervision likely tuesday (tbd). telehealth clients 2 days of your choosing.


office locations:2354 post st, san francisco, ca 943655 grand ave, oakland, ca 94610greenwood ct, orinda, ca 94563
6. . are you able to come to oakland or orinda for group supervision/training/business meeting on thursdays 11am-2pm as well as an additional hour of individual/triadic supervision virtually scheduled with your supervisor on a monday-friday? 
7. are you able to build up to 20 clients per week (onboarding 2 per week until your caseload is full. referrals will come through the practice. no need to market yourself unless you desire to)?
8. are you available during the week to get back to potential new clients for 15-minute intro calls within 24 hours of them reaching out?
9. what do you think clinically and how do you feel about rough house play with children in therapy (e.g. pillow fights, a child using a foam sword to pretend to cut off your limbs, nerf gun fights, figurine wars in which the child has you always lose by blowing up your house, killing your guy, etc)?
10. are you interested in seeing couples? are you interested in learning emotionally focused couples therapy? what training/experience with couples do you have?
11. when do you anticipate getting your license?
12. when are you available to begin?

title: intern, data science and analytics (de)
description:
hi there! we’re razorfish. we’ve been leading the marketing industry with our digital expertise since the start of the internet. but in 2020, we did a full reboot. what’s different? it all starts with people. weird, wonderful, complex people - with diverse backgrounds in strategy, creative and technology. but no matter how different we are, we all have one thing in common. we believe our differences are our strength. so we push for inclusion, challenge convention and bring in new perspectives, to inspire new ideas. because when we connect by understanding what makes people different, we can create unforgettable experiences that enrich lives. join us at razorfish.com.

we work on a hybrid model and this role will have to be on site/in person 3 days a week within our office.

job description

we are seeking a talented and motivated individual to join our team as a cloud infrastructure engineer intern. this internship offers a unique opportunity to gain hands-on experience in cloud infrastructure management and work alongside seasoned professionals in a fast-paced and collaborative environment.

responsibilities:assist in the design, implementation, and maintenance of cloud infrastructure solutions using leading-edge technologies such as aws, azure, or google cloud platform.support the deployment and configuration of virtual machines, containers, and serverless architectures to meet the needs of various projects and initiatives.collaborate with cross-functional teams to understand requirements and translate them into scalable and reliable infrastructure solutions.monitor and optimize cloud resources to ensure performance, reliability, and cost-effectiveness, leveraging automation tools and best practices.participate in troubleshooting and resolution of infrastructure issues, including incidents and outages, to minimize downtime and impact on business operations.contribute to documentation, knowledge sharing, and continuous improvement efforts to enhance the efficiency and effectiveness of cloud infrastructure management processes.

qualifications

currently pursuing a degree in computer science, information technology, or a related field.familiarity with cloud computing concepts and platforms, such as aws, azure, or google cloud platform.basic understanding of networking, security, and infrastructure-as-code principles.proficiency in scripting or programming languages such as python, powershell, or bash.strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with a passion for learning and innovation.excellent communication and teamwork abilities, with the ability to collaborate effectively in a diverse and dynamic environment.prior internship or hands-on experience in cloud computing or infrastructure management is a plus.

benefits from this internship experience:

hands-on experience working with cutting-edge cloud technologies.mentorship and guidance from experienced cloud infrastructure professionals.opportunity to contribute to real-world projects and make a meaningful impact.networking opportunities with industry professionals and fellow interns.competitive compensation package and potential for future full-time employment opportunities.eligibilityyou are a career-minded college senior, recent college graduate, career changer, or reentering the workforce after taking some time offyou are eligible to work in the u.stell us if you are available to work 40 hours per week. if not, what is your availability? we offer flexible schedules to work around school or other commitments.program duration and detailshybrid internship requiring three days in-office10-week internship program from 6/4/2024-8/9/2024; 40 hours per week

additional information

razorfish is an equal opportunity employer.

razorfish will provide accommodations to applicants needing accommodations to complete the application process.

compensation range: $20 -$23/hr. this is the pay range the company believes it will pay for this position at the time of this posting. consistent with applicable law, compensation will be determined based on the skills, qualifications, and experience of the applicant along with the requirements of the position, and the company reserves the right to modify this pay range at any time. this role may be eligible to participate in our freelancer/temporary employee medical plan through a third-party benefits administration system once certain criteria have been met. this role may also qualify for participation in our 401(k) plan after eligibility criteria have been met.

the company anticipates the application deadline for this job posting will be 03/7/2024.

title: intern - devops engineer
description:
overview

intern - devops engineer

why gm financial?

gm financial is the wholly owned captive finance subsidiary of general motors and is headquartered in fort worth, u.s. we are a global provider of auto finance solutions, with operations in north america, south america, and the asia pacific region. through our long-standing relationships with auto dealers, we offer attractive retail financing and lease programs to meet the needs of each customer. we also offer commercial lending products to dealers to help them finance and grow their businesses.

at gm financial, our team members define and shape our culture — an environment that welcomes innovative ideas, fosters integrity, and creates a sense of community and belonging. here we do more than work — we thrive.

our purpose: we pioneer the innovations that move and connect people to what matters.

our program offers

12-weeks of work experience (may 20 - aug. 9) competitive salary executive and peer networking opportunities independent and team project experiences philanthropic activities career development opportunity to apply for full-time career positions 

responsibilities

responsibilities 

devops automation engineer intern under the general direction of the global enterprise automation manager, this position will be responsible for developing solutions to enable developer productivity across the enterprise. the engineer will be tasked with the automation of security and governance controls, implementation, and maintenance of the tools and infrastructure that support the sdlc pipeline and configuration management capabilities. candidates should have a foundational knowledge of software development and system operations, familiarity with scripting languages (like python), and an understanding of automation tools (such as jenkins, ansible, or terraform). experience with version control systems, like git, is a plus. the intern will work closely with our devops team to automate and streamline operations and processes, build and maintain tools for deployment, monitoring, and operations, and troubleshoot and resolve issues in our dev, test, and production environments. interns will not only contribute but also expand their knowledge in state-of-the-art automation technologies.

job description

develop and support the tools and infrastructure that support the sdlc pipeline and configuration management capabilities.conduct problem isolation and troubleshooting on the infrastructure and tools owned by the team.communicate technical approach and implementation status to stakeholders.perform system planning, installation, and upgrades of the tools and infrastructure owned by the team.contribute to continuous improvement of the tools and capabilities of the team.

qualifications

qualifications

must be a current student pursuing a bachelor's degree or higher from an accredited college or university in computer science, engineering, or related technical field with at least one semester remaining upon start of the internship. must possess a minimum of a 3.0 (out of a 4-point scale) overall gpa. junior or senior classification preferred. familiarity with scripting languages (python).understanding of automation tools (such as jenkins, ansible, or terraform).experience with version control systems are a plus (git).strong written and verbal communication skills required. this incumbent will be required to give presentations and communicate with business leaders. strong analytical, quantitative, problem solving, and conceptual skills required. must be detail oriented and able to prioritize tasks to meet deadlines. must be able to work independently or in a team setting. previous internships are considered a plus. 

our culture: our team members define and shape our culture — an environment that welcomes innovative ideas, fosters integrity, and creates a sense of community and belonging. here we do more than work — we thrive.

compensation: competitive pay

work life balance: flexible hybrid work environment, 3-days a week in office

note: we are unable to consider candidates who require visa sponsorship for this position

title: board member
description:
volunteer: board member for animal rescue non-profit
grace animal rehabilitation center (garc) is a non-profit animal rehab center in sapulpa, oklahoma. we are dedicated to rescuing abandoned and abused cats and dogs, and placing them in loving forever homes. we also provide rehabilitation services for injured cats and dogs. our goal is to reduce the number of animals euthanized and provide a second chance for needy pets.
garc was founded in 2017 by cindy yee king, who understands abused animals because she herself was abused as a child. we are a small but dedicated team that is passionate about helping animals. we believe that while saving on dog will not change the world, that one dog’s world will be changed forever!
we rely on donations to fund our work and we are always looking for volunteers to help us care for our animals. if you are passionate about helping animals, please consider joining us as a board member.
principal responsibilities:
assist in developing policies and proceduresraise critical funds for equipment, supplies and a permanent home for our organization so we can increase the number of animals we helpact as a community ambassador in sharing our important mission with the publiceducate the public on the proper care and treatment of animalsassist in caring for our animals, to include transporting them to veterinary appointments ,participating in adoption events and day to day care of animalsassist in fundraising, marketing, finance, public relation, business management, legal and social service
qualifications:
passion for helping animals and making a difference in the lives of abandoned and abused petsexcellent communication skillscomfortable interacting with members of the public,understanding of animal care &trainingstrong fundraising and grant writing skillscreative thinker with ideas for fundraising and strategic initiativescollaborative and willing to take on a variety of tasks, from animal care to planning events to creating pr materialsability to provide a reasonable monetary donation time commitment:
10-15 hours/month or moreattending board meetings
please visit our website at https://buff.ly/43qe0bo to learn more about our mission, animals and plans for the future.

title: product marketing manager, intern
description:
job description: product marketing manager intern
position overview:we are seeking a dynamic and driven product marketing manager intern to join our team. the ideal candidate will assist in crafting engaging email campaigns aimed at increasing user engagement and retention. this role requires a blend of marketing analytics expertise, content creation skills, startup experience, and a keen understanding of user behavior. the product marketing manager intern will play a crucial role in building and analyzing user cohorts, tracking engagement metrics, and optimizing email campaigns for maximum impact.
responsibilities:email campaign management: collaborate with the marketing team to plan, execute, and optimize email campaigns aimed at enhancing user engagement and retention.content creation: design and create compelling content for email campaigns, ensuring messaging is clear, concise, and resonates with the target audience.marketing analytics: utilize analytics tools to track and analyze user engagement metrics, including open rates, click-through rates, and conversion rates. generate insights from data analysis to optimize email campaigns and drive continuous improvement.user cohort creation: work closely with the product and data teams to identify and create user cohorts based on various criteria such as behavior, demographics, and preferences.a/b testing: conduct a/b tests on email campaigns to evaluate different content, subject lines, and designs. analyze results to make data-driven decisions and refine future campaigns.startup experience: bring a startup mindset to the role, demonstrating agility, creativity, and a willingness to take initiative in a fast-paced environment.
requirements:bachelor's degree in marketing, business, or related field.2-3 years of experience in marketing analytics, preferably in a startup or tech environment.proficiency in email marketing platforms such as mailchimp, hubspot, or similar tools.strong analytical skills with the ability to interpret data and generate actionable insights.excellent written and verbal communication skills, with a talent for crafting engaging content.demonstrated ability to work collaboratively in cross-functional teams and adapt to evolving priorities.experience with cohort analysis and segmentation techniques.familiarity with basic design principles and tools for creating email templates.passion for technology and innovation, with a desire to contribute to the growth of a dynamic startup.
benefits:opportunity to gain hands-on experience in product marketing and analytics within a rapidly growing startup.mentorship from experienced marketing professionals and exposure to cutting-edge marketing strategies.flexible work schedule and the potential for remote work arrangements.competitive compensation package with potential for future growth opportunities within the company.if you meet the qualifications and are excited about the opportunity to make an impact in a dynamic startup environment, we encourage you to apply. please submit your resume and a cover letter outlining your relevant experience and why you are passionate about this role. we look forward to hearing from you!

title: internship - clinical pharmacist - home infusion
description:
iv league is one of southern california's most recognized home infusion companies. we work with prominent hospitals, physicians, oncology clinics, m.s. clinics, and surgery centers, providing i.v. and specialty medications for patients in the home or office settings. we currently have an intern pharmacist position open in our culver city office. as an intern pharmacist at iv league, you will gain hands-on experience and training in all aspects of pharmacy operations. you will work under the supervision of a licensed pharmacist to assist in the dispensing and compounding of medications, provide patient counseling, and participate in other pharmacy activities as required. this position offers the opportunity to learn about various areas of pharmacy practice, including clinical and specialty areas. if you are a motivated and dedicated pharmacy student or graduate looking to gain valuable experience and make a positive impact on patient care, we encourage you to apply for the intern pharmacist position at iv league. key responsibilities:upholds iv league’s policies and procedures and applicable state, federal and local laws.assist in the preparation, dispensing, and labeling of prescriptions according to legal and ethical standards.verifies, interprets, and reviews prescription medication orders for appropriateness, accuracy, and completeness, with special consideration given to patients with specific disease states and special age groups being served.assess patient at the initiation of drug therapy, develop a patient care plan and drug-specific monitoring regimen; monitor the patient according to the care plan throughout the course of therapy.provide patient counseling on medication usage, possible side effects, and proper storage.communicate with the patient, physician, nursing staff, pharmacists, and other members of the health care team regarding patient status, response to therapy and clinical objectives to ensure that clinical outcomes are met.support the maintenance of pharmacy inventory, including ordering and stocking medications and supplies.participate in the preparation and compounding of sterile pharmaceutical preparations.help maintain accurate patient records and prescription data in compliance with legal and regulatory standards.support medication therapy management services under the guidance of the supervising pharmacist.performs drug conversions and calculations.assemble supplies and arrange for proper delivery or pickup of home infusion medications and supplies.exhibits proper use, handling and compounding of both sterile and hazardous medications.ensure compliance with all fda, dea, state board of pharmacy, osha, and other regulatory standards.maintain a clean and organized work environment, adhering to safety and sanitation standards.attend and participate in educational programs, training, and staff meetings.perform other duties as assigned by the supervising pharmacist.
qualifications:enrolled in or recently graduated from an accredited pharmacy program.open, active, and in good standing california state intern pharmacist license.strong communication and interpersonal skills.ability to work effectively as part of a team.attention to detail and accuracy in all tasks. physical requirements:· possible prolonged periods sitting at a desk and working on a computer.· possible prolonged periods of standing and bending in cleanroom.· must be able to lift up to 15 pounds at times. work schedule:monday – sunday (rotating hours as assigned by pharmacy manager).we understand the importance of balancing work with your academic commitments. therefore, we offer a flexible work schedule tailored to your school timetable. you will have the opportunity to discuss and adjust your work hours with the pharmacy manager to ensure that your work schedule aligns with your classes and study commitments. reports to:· director of pharmacy, pharmacy manager please note this job description is not designed to cover or contain a comprehensive listing of activities, duties, or responsibilities that are required of the employee for this job. duties, responsibilities, and activities may change at any time with or without notice.
benefits (waiting period may apply for benefits):competitive compensation.opportunity for learning and growth in a dynamic pharmacy environment.mentorship from experienced pharmacists.potential for career advancement.401(k)401(k) matchinghealth insurance - for qualifying personneldental insurance - for qualifying personnelvision insurance - for qualifying personnelfsa/hsa - for qualifying personnellife insurancepaid time offreferral program
healthcare setting:inpatient
schedule:day shiftevening shiftholidaysmonday to fridayovertimeweekends as needed.
license/certification:california intern pharmacist license (required)

job title: software engineering intern
department: business solutions
reports to: vp of software engineering
job summary: our mission is to leverage creative technology in the oil and gas industry to eliminate emissions and move us towards a more sustainable future. we are enabling that mission by streaming, processing, structuring, and reporting on a wide variety of data from both our vapor recovery units and our drone fleets. leveraging the latest in cloud computing and technology, we build the systems that power all of our operations. are you a student that is looking to leverage software to make an impact on the world? title: risk management internship
description:
washington cities insurance authority (wcia) is a municipal organization of cities, towns and interlocal government entities in washington state that join together for the purpose of providing liability and property protection to its member entities. wcia provides members with comprehensive liability coverage (auto, general, police, public officials, and errors & omissions), equipment breakdown, crime & fidelity, property and auto physical damage. wcia also provides risk management services to reduce the amount and frequency of member losses and to decrease the cost incurred by members in the handling and litigation of claims.
wcia is recruiting qualified students interested in pursuing their knowledge in the risk management, public risk pooling/insurance field while gaining a broad perspective of public property and casualty exposures facing local governments. during the program the individual will work closely and rotate through wcia’s key departments: risk management, claims, underwriting/insurance services, and member services department. the individual will get the opportunity to assist senior risk management representatives with annual field visits and liability best practice audits associated with local government exposures ranging from; police, special events, fleet and other unique exposures. the hiring level is entry level students seeking a career in risk management or the insurance industry. responsibilities include conducting loss analysis, evaluating public liability loss exposures, and recommending controls, organizing data, work on claim forms by type of loss and look to correct previous data entry issues. the individual should be a creative thinker, problem solver, skilled with microsoft office products, strong presenter with the ability to communicate confidently with senior level leaders and desire to learn. the intern will need to develop a broad understanding of the liability loss environment facing local government agencies. the individual will become familiar with wcia’s risk management information system origami and assist in analyzing the membership’s reported $16 billion worth of scheduled properties insured by wcia and analyze loss data for frequency and severity issues.
job location: hybrid work arrangement, with approximately one to two days per month at our tukwila, wa office and other days working remotely from your washington residence or traveling to members across the state of washington.
position salary range: $20 per hour, 40 hours per week, expected to last for three months.
closing date: open until filled.
candidate must possess strong verbal and written communication skills, good organizational ability, and be able to build positive working relationship with the membership. the individual that we are seeking must be customer focused, analytical, able to multi-task, and have good time management skills. degree or pursuing a degree in loss control/risk management, occupational safety, insurance, finance or related field. strong computer skills also preferred. regular day and an occasional overnight travel may be required. valid washington state driver’s license and reliable transportation required.

title: online efl intern teacher
description:
plus company overviewfor over 45 years, plus has provided english language courses to international students from across the globe. we work tirelessly so our students can experience american culture, as well as learn and develop in a vibrant, educational and safe environment. with dedicated teachers and leaders, we create a positive, fun and inclusive atmosphere which stretches far beyond the classroom. plus started operating in the efl market in 1972. today, our group with offices in london, beijing, new york, and milan represents one of the world's largest and finest organizations in language teaching.
live online courses overviewour live online courses are non-credit bearing and are designed to help our international students improve their english-speaking skills. the majority of our students are high school-aged.
online instruction occurs in a live video format (on zoom) so that students can interact with their instructor and peers, doing partner and group work and participating in all-class discussions. plus is offering an opportunity for teacher trainees to gain experience teaching online: to fulfill teaching practice (practicum/internship) requirements for teaching certificate or master's programs, to gain more virtual classroom experience to further their teaching career, and to have the opportunity to observe professionally-taught lessons in conjunction with teaching practice.
as an online intern teacher, you will teach speaking-focused english as a foreign language lessons to groups of international students. plus will provide you with the following:training on how to teach effectively in an online setting.the curriculum and full lesson plans. it may be possible to design your own lesson plans based on plus's lesson objectives if required by your certificate- or degree-awarding institution (lesson plan approval required).the opportunity to record the lessons, if needed, to allow for self-reflection and/or institutional feedback.additional, recorded lessons taught by professional instructors for your observation (if needed).a certificate at the completion of your practice experience that includes the number of practice teaching hours completed.
scheduleeach course is 10 hours and are held as one-hour or two-hour classes. some may be held weekly, some may be held twice a week, etc. over the course of several weeks.classes are generally held between the hours of 10:00am-2:30pm est / 7:00am-11:30am pst on weekdays.
interns are scheduled based on their availability. class days, times, and weeks are subject to enrollment.
minimum time requirementthe internship length is a minimum of 15 hours, including at least 5 hours of observing classes taught by professional teachers and at least 10 hours of teaching practice. because this is an unpaid internship, students are encouraged to consult with their faculty advisor to determine if this opportunity is eligible for academic credit. if fulfilling required practice hours for a master's or certificate program, teaching hour requirements may vary by institution.
intern responsibilitiesdeliver well-prepared online english as a foreign language lessons to diverse classrooms of international students in a professional and engaging mannerreview the provided curriculum to adequately prepare for each lesson; prepare to supplement the curriculum with additional materials, as appropriatepractice appropriate classroom management techniques to ensure a safe, comfortable, and engaging learning environment for all studentscollaborate with the head office regarding instructional methods and the curriculumcomplete online training and attend an orientation sessiontake attendance dailymaintain frequent and professional communication with the head office
qualifications and desired skillsmust be 18 years or olderenrollment in or completion of teacher training program strongly preferredbachelor’s degree in process or completedcommand of zoom preferredcommand of google suite preferred
minimum technical requirementswebcam (hdd preferred)4 mbps download and upload internet speed.intel i5 processor or equivalent4gb computer rammicrophone and speaker (noise canceling headphones preferred)

title: nbcu news group creative & design internships – academic year
description:
company description

if you think all the fun only happens in summer, think again! nbcuniversal has expanded our academic year internship program. as an academic year intern, you’ll get immersed in exciting work, experience our unique nbcu culture, and feel the real support and guidance for 2 whole semesters.

the nbcuniversal internship program allows students to unlock their potential by contributing to big projects in meaningful ways that matter. to thrive as an nbcu intern, you are ready to bring your passion for media, entertainment, and technology along with your desire to learn each day.

in addition to our internships being paid, we will also offer the following for our academic year interns:

paid time off for mental health, school exams, and personal holidays.robust networking, learning and development opportunities.complimentary peacock subscription during your internship.free admission to universal parks to use during your internship.access to mental health resources including counseling sessions.

academic year recruitment timeline

april 8 – april 26: applications open (may close early due to applicant volume)

mid april – late may: first round video interviews

early – mid june: second round interviews

late june – early july: selected candidates receive offers

job description

academic year internship program:

applications close: april 26th at 11:59pm et. pro tip: submit your application as soon as possible – applications could close earlier due to applicant volume! program dates: september 16, 2024 – april 18, 2025time commitment: 16-24 hours per week. exact schedule to be determined based on business need. we will do our best to accommodate course schedules.format: opportunities listed will require an intern to work from new york, ny.

to apply

submit a one-page resume that outlines your work experience, accomplishments, and your graduation month and year.submit a cover letter that highlights your passion for nbcuniversal, the locations (listed below) you are open to for internships, and your top areas of interest (opportunities listed below).upload a transcript (unofficial transcripts are fine) that confirms your status as an actively enrolled student in a degree-granting program.

more americans watch nbc news, msnbc and cnbc than any news organization in the world. nbc news’ leading and award-winning television news broadcasts include today, nbc nightly news with lester holt, meet the press with chuck todd and dateline nbc. msnbc is the #2 network across all of cable and is the premiere destination for breaking news, award-winning content, in-depth analysis and informed perspectives. cnbc is the recognized world leader in business news and provides real-time financial market coverage, business and general news content, and primetime reality programming, consumed by more than 557 million people per month across all platforms. the rapidly-growing nbc news digital group – including its streaming news services nbc news now and today all day – and the network’s award-winning original podcast and audio unit provide continuous content to consumers wherever they are, whenever they want it. 

areas of placement may include, but are not limited to:

nbc news digital art & photo

the nbc news digital art and photo department works with the editorial departments across our newsrooms to photo edit, illustrate, and create visual design work for our sites, apps, and social platforms. this intern will work with art directors, reporters, and editors to select and edit images for articles on today.com, msnbc.com, and/or nbcnews.com, and our digital platforms. they will produce conceptual, photo-based illustrations with photoshop and illustrator and design and produce social graphics.

nbc news artworks

this intern will learn the process and support the daily graphic production for various news programs including today, nightly news, msnbc and special events. we are looking for interns majoring in graphic design or animation with experience in adobe suite and cinema 4d.

qualifications

basic requirements:

must be actively enrolled in a degree-granting program at an accredited institution during the entire duration of the program (september 2024 through april 2025). december 2024 graduates are not eligible for our 2024-2025 academic year program.current class standing of sophomore or above (30 credits).must be authorized to work in the united states throughout the entire duration of the internship (september 2024 to april 2025). visa sponsorship is not available for this position.must be available to work 16-24 hours per week from september 16, 2024 – april 18, 2025.must be willing to work in new york, ny.

desired characteristics

students with class standing of junior or above preferred.cumulative gpa of 3.0 or above.strong interest in the media industry.     commitment to building an inclusive work environment. previous internship experience and on-campus involvement. strong interest in and demonstrated knowledge of current affairs. technical skills: adobe creative suite, cinema 4d, strong knowledge of photoshop and illustrator. desired majors: digital/new media studies, communications, graphic design, animation. 

the hourly rate for student interns is $17.00.  

information for international students attending u.s. universities

to participate in nbcuniversal’s 2024-2025 academic year internship program, you must confirm at the time of interview that you will be able to provide us work authorization documentation for the entire duration of the internship. since the nbcu academic year internship program spans the traditional “fall” and “spring” semesters, you should talk to the dso at your school’s international students services office about your need to apply for cpt work authorization twice to ensure you are authorized to work both semesters and throughout the entirety of the program.

please also note that future visa sponsorship at nbcuniversal is rarely available for post-grad employment opportunities.

nbcuniversal is an e-verify company, requiring all employees to have an active social security number. any candidate receiving an internship offer must complete form i-9 with their social security number within 3 days of hire.

additional information

nbcuniversal's policy is to provide equal employment opportunities to all applicants and employees without regard to race, color, religion, creed, gender, gender identity or expression, age, national origin or ancestry, citizenship, disability, sexual orientation, marital status, pregnancy, veteran status, membership in the uniformed services, genetic information, or any other basis protected by applicable law. nbcuniversal will consider for employment qualified applicants with criminal histories in a manner consistent with relevant legal requirements, including the city of los angeles fair chance initiative for hiring ordinance, where applicable.

if you are a qualified individual with a disability or a disabled veteran, you have the right to request a reasonable accommodation if you are unable or limited in your ability to use or access nbcunicareers.com as a result of your disability. you can request reasonable accommodations in the us by calling 1-818-777-4107 and in the uk by calling +44 2036185726.

overview: the it intern at surge staffing will play a pivotal role in supporting the it department across various functions. this position offers a unique opportunity to gain hands-on experience in all aspects of it and understand their significance in a growing organization. the intern will collaborate closely with teams in technical support, systems administration, asset management, and deployment coordination.

location: full-time (monday - friday) on-site position at surge staffing hq in columbus, ohio.

responsibilities:partner with mentors in each it-related role, providing support in their specific duties.serve as the first line of support for any information technology issues and problems.provide assistance via phone, email, or ticket management system, tracking issues to resolution.support computers, laptops, tablets, smartphones, and applications.escalate problems to tier 2 and tier 3 support teams as required.follow up on tickets at pre-defined intervals until resolved, acting as a liaison between customers and technical escalation teams.conduct audits on file system permissions and raspberry pi devices across the company, reporting status and age.manage active directory and exchange email accounts.provide network and local printer support and troubleshooting.undertake additional tasks or projects to broaden understanding of it and office operations.

qualifications:at least a junior pursuing a bachelor's degree in it or a related field.excellent verbal and written communication skills.exceptional interpersonal and customer service abilities.strong organizational skills with meticulous attention to detail.proficiency in microsoft office, outlook, exchange administration, and related software.strong analytical and problem-solving skills.ability to work independently as well as collaboratively within a team.methodical approach to problem-solving using appropriate tools.demonstrated ability to maintain confidentiality and exercise discretion and good judgment.

company overview: surge is a renowned national leader with over 50 years of experience providing top-quality staffing and innovative workforce solutions. staffing flexibility has become integral to meeting the evolving supply and demand of our economy. surge specializes in offering flexible, time-saving, and cost-effective workforce solutions tailored to each unique working environment. we are committed to delivering quality service, ensuring our customers receive nothing but the best from order placement to assignment completion. from traditional, temporary, temp-to-hire, direct hire placements, recruiting, and msp, all our solutions are designed with our customers in mind to ultimately reduce costs, turnover, and increase productivity.

equal opportunity employer: surge is proud to be an equal opportunity employer, fostering diversity and inclusion in the workplace. we value and embrace the unique contributions of all individuals, regardless of race, color, religion, sex, disability, age, sexual orientation, national origin, veteran status, genetic information, or any other protected status. we are committed to providing access, equal opportunity, and reasonable accommodation for individuals with disabilities in employment, services, programs, and activities.

for more information, visit www.surgestaffing.com.

title: summer data scientist internship
description:
lending science dm, inc., the leader in financial services marketing and analytics seeks a data analyst in the financial services industry. this candidate must be a fast learner, dependable, energetic team player with a positive attitude. ideal candidate will be in the latter part of their program pursuing degrees in data science, industrial engineering, mathematics, statistics or relevant degrees.this internship requires a 20 hour per week commitment with 2-3 days in an office environment and remote access during the remainder of the week. this will be a valuable experience for any student pursuing their career. this is a compensated internship. 
responsibilities include and are not limited to:
interpreting data, analyzing results using statistical techniques and providing ongoing reports. acquiring data from primary or secondary data sources and maintaining databases/data systems.identifying, analyzing, and interpreting trends or patterns in complex data sets.filtering and “cleaning” data by reviewing computer reports, printouts, and performance indicators to locate and correct code problems.working with management to prioritize business and information needs.locating and defining new process improvement opportunities.using tableau and visualization packages to create and support kpi and operations dashboards ad-hoc report writing. using advanced excel function or coding languages to support data analytics.

title: engineering intern
description:
overview

 join us as we rise to the challenge 

at kci, we’re building an enduring company that provides unparalleled value to our employee-owners. we make our mark designing and delivering our world-class solutions, so we invest deeply in developing our team. we reward integrity and commitment, and when we do well, you do well. our people have freedom to innovate, unlimited growth, a voice that matters, a lifestyle that works, and skin in the game. because life’s more fun when you own it.

the company

kci technologies, inc. is a 100% employee-owned engineering, consulting and construction firm serving clients throughout the united states. kci is recognized as an industry leader, employing cutting-edge technologies, management practices and strategic growth initiatives. employee ownership fosters an entrepreneurial spirit, encourages technical expertise, and shapes strategic planning.

kci is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace dedicated to pursuing and hiring a diverse workforce.

duties, responsibilities & other

kci is looking for bright and highly motivated environmental engineering students (rising juniors, rising seniors or rising graduate students) that are intereseted in water/wastewater or related field.

the intern will work under the supervision of a technical manager within one of kci's many diverse consulting, engineering, scientific, or design practices to gain exposure to real world projects and daily work tasks. summer interns will work a 40-hour week, with at least 10 weeks of work required during the period from mid-may to the end of august. interns will be assigned a dedicated supervisor and mentor, are required to attend planned activities, and must submit mid-summer and end-of-summer reports/presentation s.

duties may include

 enrolled in a college engineering program.  assist in the development of plans, quantities and cost estimates  conduct data analysis and reporting  complete field surveys or laboratory assignments  conduct entry level engineering, design or drafting  prepare engineering studies and coordinate design plans with other design disciplines  apply quality control procedures in alignment with iso.  communicate with client(s) on a limited basis  provide on-time delivery of project expectations with favorable feedback  perform administrative or office management duties. 

qualifications

 enrolled in a college engineering program.  microstation or autocad skills desired but not required.  no experience required.  minimum gpa 3.0  pre-employment drug screening is a condition of employment. background and motor vehicle checks may be required based upon position. 

 performance expectations 

 complete annual individual training program  comply with all kci policies and procedures  coordinate with other practices and sub-consultants as required  ensure projects fulfill iso compliance requirements  complete other duties as required/assigned  achieve or exceed budgeted individual utilization target(85% typical)  attend bi-weekly(or more frequently as necessary) meetings with project team on all projects/tasks  complete all tasks on time and within budget while fully meeting the scope  operate within specified (corporate or project) budget parameters  assist with preliminary concept designs and submit for approval  compare field data to base plan  conduct site visits and gather data, including measurements, sketches, notes and photographs as needed  prepare design calculations, drawings, specifications and cost estimates for review and approval  review and approve shop drawings 

kci technologies, inc. is an equal opportunity employer. all qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability or veteran status.

title: marketing communications internship
description:
marketing communication internship | gpg is looking for a candidate who is enthusiastic about marketing communications at a nonprofit. this individual can work independently with minimal supervision and collaboratively with others as this is a creative position that requires ongoing communication with the development team. ideal interns have a self-starting and open-minded attitude and can work under tight deadlines. ideal candidates will also have demonstrated professional or classroom experience working in both traditional (e.g., print ads, direct mail) and digital (e.g., email, social media, website) marketing. the marketing communication intern will assist the gpg marketing and development team in a variety of creative initiatives including: 
graphic design: duties include, but are not limited to developing graphics, videos, and photographs for digital marketing channels, as well as print collateral materials (e.g., brochures, flyers, etc...). knowledge of graphic design software like canva or adobe indesign, photoshop preferred. gpg has a free premium canva account that can be used as adobe photoshop.
social media: duties include, but are not limited to developing social media posts (copy and images/videos) for facebook, instagram, and tiktok. knowledge of scheduling software(s) and ability to update a master social media calendar. 
copywriting: duties include, but are not limited to writing and developing copy for digital marketing channels, as well as print materials like ads and press releases.
events: duties include, but are not limited to assisting with event-related project needs as required by the development director. this could include helping with on-site logistics, volunteer recruitment, social media, or other preparations. interest in providing administrative support for fundraising events critical to raising needed funds to continue saving homeless animals.
qualifications | experience with special events email marketing, social media content creation and knowledge of platforms, specifically facebook, instagram, and tiktok trends is preferred. an understanding of social analytics, paid social advertising, and video editing, as well as website management, preferably wordpress is a plus. adhering to branding guidelines, having excellent grammar skills and handling multiple projects simultaneously with minimal supervision is a must.
in order to review your experience, please share links to your professional examples, relevant class assignments, and/or social media accounts when submitting your cover letter/resume. please submit all materials to alison erazmus, development and marketing manager, at alison@gatewaypets.org
about internship program | gpg offers unpaid internships for students currently enrolled in a two- or four-year program, as well as individuals who have recently graduated with an associate or bachelor’s degree. interns should share gpg’s passion for providing judgment-free, equitable access to pet resources and removing barriers to pet ownership. all applicants must attend a new volunteer orientation and conduct themselves in a professional manner consistent with the company handbook. gpg strives to create an environment for interns that fosters ethics, learning, and mentorship while developing a deeper understanding and practice of community service. interns will be provided the opportunity to learn about other areas of animal welfare through job shadowing or informational meetings with other departments.
about organization | gateway pet guardians (gpg) is leading the animal welfare industry by investing in people and communities to end the cycle of animal homelessness in the east side pet district - east st. louis, cahokia heights, washington park, and fairmont city. by building capacity for animal advocates within the community, gpg increases animal well-being across the east side pet district and beyond. gpg is proudly located in east st. louis, joining an already robust community of pet lovers. by removing barriers to foster and adoption, and providing an infusion of resources such as affordable, quality veterinary care and pet supplies – gpg unleashes the power of partnership! the dedicated and experienced professionals at gpg put their heart and soul into preventing owned pets from entering an already crowded shelter system. staff, volunteers and supporters of gpg believe everyone deserves the love of a pet, regardless of zip code, income, employment status, or housing. by reimagining what animal rescue really means, gpg goes above and beyond to ensure equitable access to resources so that no one in the community has to make the impossible choice to give up their beloved pet.

position description

the international tax manager is responsible for minimizing united states (u.s.) federal taxes on worldwide income and legal entity income taxes through effective tax planning and ensuring income tax returns are prepared in a manner that complies with the tax laws. this position will minimize u.s./foreign tax assessments by directly supporting our positions and foreign subsidiaries with defending their cross-border activity (transfer pricing) during external tax audits. ensure federal international tax expense is properly determined and reported in accordance with u.s. generally accepted accounting principles (gaap). this position is used to develop future potential tax leadership.

principal responsibilities

 review, prepare or provide technical expertise on u.s. international compliance including, gilti, fdii, beat, subpart f, form 1118, form 5471, e&p, ptep and any other u.s. international compliance. review quarterly projections of u.s. tax impact of international transactions to ensure prepared in accordance with asc 740 income tax calculations. review and analyze country-by-country report pillar two global minimum tax and other beps reporting requirements. maintain an understanding of laws and regulations for tax reporting implications and can present concise conclusions on tax technical issues as required. monitor and ensure compliance with global transfer pricing policies and inter company activity. assist with tax audits and related matters (income tax, transfer pricing). ensures tax processes comply with sox requirements. assist with special projects as required. management and development of assigned team members.

required experience and education

 10 or more years of experience preferably in corporate tax compliance in a public accounting, manufacturing or consumer goods industry knowledge of international/federal income tax law, international tax provision and u.s. international tax compliance calculations, tax treaties, transfer pricing with large multinational corporation research complex international tax issues extensive knowledge of u.s. gaap deferred tax accounting rules strong oral and written communication skills demonstrate ethical and moral behavior, act with integrity able to analyze and grasp a situation quickly from different perspectives and create well-reasoned solutions experience working in a team environment

desired personal skills/attributes/and qualifications

 master's degree in accounting, or finance, cpa, master of tax (mba, llm or other advanced degree) visible and passionate leader develops team members through coaching and feedback highly skilled computer literacy (i.e., sap, corptax, excel, word and powerpoint)

title: corporate internships - summer 2024
description:
why cha? 

 finding a better way   .  it’s what we do, in creating solutions, helping people, and improving our world.

imagine yourself at cha, tackling some of the world’s most critical challenges – around clean water, safe roads, efficient energy, resilient power sources, and buildings. imagine yourself contributing your skills and passion to creating a world that is more sustainable, more diverse, and one that leans forward towards a better future.

our people are approachable, caring, down to earth, plugged in, and hands-on. they are skilled, thoughtful, and innovative. can you imagine yourself here?

cha is looking for someone with your passion to join our  2024 summer internship program!  if you are earning your degree in  information technology and are interested in an internship working in one of our many disciplines, apply today to join us in  finding a better way   . 

 your impact 

a cha internship will ensure you are ready to make the transition from student to professional. over our 10-week program, you will have plenty of opportunities to  connect  with people who will help you on your career journey. you will connect with your peers through group projects, planned social events and working alongside one another in the office or field. you will connect with professionals across the business, collaborating on a company projects and important work that will have a real, positive impact on the world we all live in. the connections you make during your cha internship can play a critical role as you complete your studies and transition into your professional career.

cha's internship program aims to  prepare  students for the professional working environment through opportunities to work as part of a team, developing the planning skills to effectively balance work demands with your personal life, and figuring out how to navigate different personalities to ensure team success.

our program is more than just an internship; you will learn about yourself; identify your career passions and go after them. as a cha intern you will experience working in your field - there is nothing like real world experience! an internship with cha will help you clarify and bring into focus your future career goals, it will give you the opportunity to try new things, explore and  discover  what you want for your future.

so, join us for summer 2024; let’s find a better way together!

want to learn more about how cha is  finding a better way  ? visit us online at chasolutions.com .

requirements

 information technology 

 experience with microsoft office required.  experience with full stack web development preferred  java, python and sql experience are a plus  must have ability to work in a team environment.  good oral and written communication skills required. 

may 2024 graduates will  not  be accepted for cha's internship program.

 culture/eeo statement 

at cha, we work every day to create solutions, help people, and improve our world, committed to creating and fostering excellence in our diverse and highly talented teams. our teams continually strive to find better ways – always searching, never settling – to achieve extraordinary results. our values around hiring, training, and community engagement reflect a company culture that is inclusive and forward leaning, always pushing the limits of what is possible.

 we as an organization celebrate the values of inclusion and equality, and advocate for the full participation of all people in an environment free of discrimination. to support these values, we invite all qualified applicants to be considered for employment regardless of race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, citizenship, age, disability, protected veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by law.

title: executive director for coordination of student crisis & safety intervention
description:
summary of position

executive director for coordination of student crisis and safety intervention

the executive director for coordination of student crisis and safety intervention will be responsible for the intersection of key decisions for the following psu teams: title ix, student life and university police department, by leveraging their expertise to minimize the risk that occurs in situations that could evolve into a crisis level event for any given student. the position will help to create, implement and evaluate various procedures and protocols to address issues amongst the aforementioned teams. this position is responsible for providing and arranging support, training, and communication to promote improved collaboration across conduct, student life, police service and mental health, and to enhance efficacy and efficiency. this will include the planning and future instatement of a crisis co-response model with appropriate university, law enforcement and community resources involved to effectively aid in addressing mental health crises and ongoing quality of life social issues within the university and its local community, this position will also develop models that efficiently address problems and issues in this area including such things as peer counseling and mentoring. the position will work closely with upd and assist with potential case or individual patient follow up for community services support, on an as needed basis. the individual will work collaboratively with other stakeholders to assure tenets are met, and nurture steering committee and community support for these endeavors. the position will spearhead collaboration from faculty and staff as selective expertise may be sought to ensure success with position endeavors in the various teams across campus.

work is performed under general supervision of the vice president of finance and administration and is guided by plymouth state university policies and procedures, quality assurance, emergency mental health and diagnostic guidelines and professional practices, and including ethical standards related to client interactions, judicial directives, law enforcement policies and procedures, state and federal licensure and accreditation regulations and guidelines they are expected to perform duties as specified, and in conjunction and alignment with, the policies and procedures listed above.

this position does require work during the evenings, weekends and must be available to report to work at any time. the position will be tasked with collaborating with other entities on campus to ensure appropriate coverage exists to respond to a crisis situation. peak evenings and weekends work will occur in conjunction with psu’s academic year calendar.

please note a priority applicant review deadline of may 1st.

other minimum qualifications

bachelor’s degree in social work, psychology, criminal justice or other social service-related field and at least 2 years of related work experience within the public behavioral health or criminal justice system.clinical licensure in good standingknowledge of laws and legal procedures relating to the emergency mental health system to include knowledge and familiarity with nh state emergency custody order/ temporary detention order issuance and process.familiarity with issues affecting those with mh, sud , dd/id disorders.strong communication and interpersonal skills and demonstrated ability to represent and promote the program within the agency and the community.ability to develop, prepare, and present training and educational materials.demonstrated ability to prepare and maintain detailed records and prepare related reports.

title: sales underwriting intern
description:
otr solutions is an innovator in the transportation industry providing a suite of factoring, fuel, and business management focused solutions. we help new and established companies get fast access to the funds they need for daily operations. as a private equity backed fintech company, we are looking to grow our best-in-class financial organization. 
otr has been recognized as a “top workplace” by the atlanta journal-constitution since 2016!
as an sales underwriting intern, you will be supporting members of the team ranging from new employees up to management. the sales underwriting department is responsible for analyzing risk and evaluating financial integrity for potential new clients. 
responsibilities: review and file all paperwork for prospective new clients and communicate missing items to our sales team make email verification calls to prospective clients as a form of fraud prevention assist senior team members with verifying pay statuses and load delivery information for active buyouts enter client data and activate new accounts in our system process a ucc filing for each client communicate with the team about the activity of daily operations assist team members and management as otherwise needed 
what we look for: working towards bachelor's degree in business or related field exceptional communication, organizational, and problem-solving skills team player with multi-tasking and prioritizing abilities strong analytical skills and good judgement ability to handle difficult conversations proficiency in ms excel and related computer knowledge 
otr’s mission is to create exceptional value for our clients by providing industry leading financing and back-office solutions delivered through a blend of outstanding customer service and tech-driven efficiencies, while maintaining a culture that provides the opportunity for employees to achieve greatness. 
otr solutions is an equal opportunity employer

title: seasonal intern: cell therapy customer support & data maintenance
description:
job details

level

entry

job location

minneapolis mn - minneapolis, mn

position type

internship

education level

bachelor's degree

travel percentage

none

job shift

day
